races.race_name: Specialist Group Novice Hurdle
Close Brothers Irish EBF Mares Novice Hurdle (Listed Race)

runners.name: Future Prospect
runners.spotlight: Smart bumper winner but had a tendency to race keenly; got off the mark over hurdles on debut at Naas but was pulled-up in the Mares' novice at Cheltenham; a better effort in a Fairyhouse Mares' Grade 1 since over 2m4f and a repeat back to this trip would see her go well.
runners.comment: Pulled-up at Cheltenham but better in a Fairyhouse Grade 1 since over 2m4f; this trip suit

runners.name: Blue Velvet
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner got off the mark over hurdles (2m3f) at this track in January; not a bad run at Cheltenham next time but was pulled-up at Fairyhouse since; isn't the yard's first-string and has to rebound.
runners.comment: Was pulled-up at Fairyhouse last time; isn't the yard's first-string and has to rebound

runners.name: Alliteration
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner last autumn got off the mark over hurdles in a Fairyhouse maiden last time on fourth start in this sphere; the cheekpieces worn then are retained but she has to do more in this grade.
runners.comment: Won a Fairyhouse maiden last time; the cheekpieces worn then are retained; has to do more

runners.name: Adrienne
runners.spotlight: Flat maiden has taken well to hurdles, winning a Fairyhouse maiden on debut and running some fine races in defeat since; has to find more to win but should be in the mix.
runners.comment: Fine runs in defeat in strong company; has to find more to win but should be in the mix

runners.name: Catchabird
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough when winning a Wexford maiden hurdle last month before following up in a Navan handicap four days later; however, carries a double penalty and is the lowest-rated in this field so has a tough task on.
runners.comment: Carries a double penalty and is the lowest-rated in this field so has a tough task on

runners.name: Diamond Du Berlais
runners.spotlight: Failed to win over hurdles in France but showed useful form; won over 2m5f at Ludlow on debut for this yard and ran a cracker in the Mares' Novice at Cheltenham since over this trip; is the one to beat if reproducing that form.
runners.comment: Fine run in the Mares' Novice at Cheltenham; is the one to beat if reproducing that form

races.race_name: Mongey Communications La Touche Cup Cross Country Chase

runners.name: The Goffer
runners.spotlight: Fourth behind Stumptown in the 2025 Glenfarclas Chase; went on to finish third in the Topham and fifth in this race; twice second over the shorter course here this season, behind Desertmore House in November and behind Vanillier in February; may rebound after below-par effort at Cheltenham.
runners.comment: Well below best at Cheltenham, solid course form linked to Desertmore House and Vanillier

runners.name: Busselton
runners.spotlight: Beat Desertmore House in this event in last year when trained by Joseph O'Brien, for whom he won five chases in all, including the 2022 Kerry National; well below best when fifth behind Desertmore House here in November, tailed off at Cheltenham in November, had a first spin for Enda Bolger over hurdles and an inadequate trip at Cork; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Last year's winner, this season's form a cause for concern, change of scene may work

runners.name: Desertmore House
runners.spotlight: Close second to Busselton here last year on cross-country debut; looked in fine shape when beating The Goffer and subsequent Cheltenham winner Final Orders over 3m here in November; pulled up over hurdles the following month; dropped away from the 23rd of 31 obstacles at Cheltenham; obvious chance if on song again.
runners.comment: Second in this last year, went one better in November, disappointing run at Cheltenham
runners.owner: O B P Carroll & Anthony Vaughan

runners.name: Vanillier
runners.spotlight: Second in the 2023 Grand National; third in successive editions of the Glenfarclas at the Cheltenham Festival, last year when around 8l behind stablemate Stumptown, this year 20l behind another stablemate Final Orders; remote sixth behind Desertmore House here in November; much livelier display when beating The Goffer in February, major chance.
runners.comment: Beat The Goffer here in February, third at Cheltenham, retains the ability to feature

runners.name: Dorans Law
runners.spotlight: Stays well, won a 3m2f handicap hurdle at Ballinrobe two seasons ago; won a chase on good ground at Wexford last summer; pulled up in the Ulster National a month ago on first run since a 12l second at the Listowel festival; first attempt at this course.
runners.comment: Stays well and is effective in handicaps at a modest level, first attempt at this course

runners.name: Bodhisattva
runners.spotlight: Third of five finishers in the 3m7f Edinburgh National at Musselburgh in February, not as good as his second in the same race in 2025; weak handicap form lately; well beaten in previous attempts at this course.
runners.comment: Not at his best on the track this season, well beaten in previous attempts at this course

runners.name: Lough Derg Spirit
runners.spotlight: Useful hunter chase form at the age of 11 in 2023, third in the Aintree Foxhunter and in the championship event at this meeting; has been struggling this season after a long absence.
runners.comment: Useful hunter chase form at the age of 11 in 2023, struggling this season after a layoff

runners.name: Shannon Royale
runners.spotlight: Won a 3m beginners' chase for Gordon Elliott last season; lost form and has failed to show anything like his best since joining this team; has reportedly bled, including when a remote seventh behind Vanillier over this course in February.
runners.comment: Has reportedly bled, including when a remote seventh behind Vanillier here in February

runners.name: Outside The Door
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner, including four over fences; ran a fine race over the orthodox fences when second in a 3m7f handicap at last season's festival; made all to win a Wexford handicap last August before pulling up in the Kerry National; remote seventh behind Desertmore House here in November.
runners.comment: Useful staying handicap chaser, remote seventh behind Desertmore House here in November

runners.name: Cavalry Master
runners.spotlight: Veteran six-time winner; a shadow of his former self in points and cross-country races, though not disgraced at 100-1 in a handicap chase at Galway last September.
runners.comment: Veteran six-time winner, a shadow of his former self in points and cross-country races
runners.owner: Peter Maher & M J Halligan & Jeffrey Brennan & Alan E Smith

runners.name: Vital Island
runners.spotlight: Winner of this race in 2023 and won over a shorter trip here last year, two days after a gallant fourth behind Busselton in this race; also a former winner of the Ladies' Cup; a proper stalwart here and over the banks at Lingstown, but the years are catching up.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2023, a proper stalwart here and at Lingstown, the years are catching up

runners.name: Walking On Glass
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose sort; 4-38 over jumps, 4-30 on Flat; 66-1 when in rear throughout in the race won by Desertmore House here in November.
runners.comment: 66-1 when in rear throughout in the race won by Desertmore House here in November

runners.name: Benny The Duke
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler; 0-10 in points, remote seventh in this race last year; no apparent chance.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler, 0-10 in points, remote seventh in this race last year, no apparent chance

runners.name: The Bosses Oscar
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler for Gordon Elliott in his prime, three wins as well as second in the 2021 Pertemps Final; also runner-up to Busselton over fences at Listowel later that year; has won only once from 16 starts in points; out of sorts since second to Desertmore House at Damma House last November.
runners.comment: Poor form in points since second to Desertmore House at Damma House last November

races.race_name: Conway Piling Handicap Hurdle (Listed Race)

runners.name: Fillyoureye
runners.spotlight: Kilbeggan bumper winner a year ago got off the mark over hurdles when easily winning a 2m7f maiden here in February on heavy ground; ran too freely when pulled-up as favourite at Fairyhouse since; has a shout if settling better.
runners.comment: Ran too freely when pulled-up as fav' at Fairyhouse last time; a shout if settling better
runners.owner: Mrs N Flynn & John Joseph Flynn

runners.name: Seaniecon
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner last year got off the mark over hurdles over 3m1f at Hereford in heavy ground; was well beaten on livelier ground in an Aintree Grade 1 last time and has to do much better; cheekpieces may help.
runners.comment: Well beaten on lively ground in Aintree Grade 1 last time; cheekpieces may help now

runners.name: The Lovely Man
runners.spotlight: Hit form since joining this yard, winning twice over fences including the Leinster National at Naas; also won over hurdles at Gowran; this ground is faster than ideal but his hurdles mark is appealing.
runners.comment: Two chase wins and one over hurdles this term; ground faster than ideal;  appealing mark

runners.name: Ballykinlar
runners.spotlight: Won three consecutive handicaps last summer up to 2m4f for Donncha Duggan; some fine runs in defeat after including since switched to this yard; has to prove he stays this trip.
runners.comment: Three wins last summer; some fine runs in defeat after; has to prove he stays this trip

runners.name: Fad Eadrainn
runners.spotlight: Is useful on his day as when beating some decent sorts in a Fairyhouse conditions hurdle in November; best run since when beaten 10l in a 2m5f Fairyhouse handicap last time; this trip is a query.
runners.comment: Not bad run when beaten 10l in a 2m5f Fairyhouse handicap last time; this trip is a query

runners.name: Onewaywest
runners.spotlight: Improved sort this season, winning handicaps at Warwick and Wetherby despite jumping to his right; wasn't at his best in the Pertemps Final at Cheltenham, but the return to a right-handed track should suit so considered.
runners.comment: Not at his best in the Pertemps Final; back at a right-handed track should suit; contender

runners.name: Blue Mosque
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper and Cork maiden hurdle in 2024 but no win since; however, she has run well in defeat several times up to 2m7f including at this track; up 2lb from last time and one to consider.
runners.comment: No win since 2024 but has run well in defeat several times including here; one to consider

runners.name: One Big Boum
runners.spotlight: Won his fourth hurdle race when a wide-margin winner at Fairyhouse in February; found 2m too sharp off this mark at Wexford last time and this will suit better, so not without a chance.
runners.comment: Found 2m too sharp off this mark at Wexford last time and this will suit better; a chance

runners.name: Buachaillbocht
runners.spotlight: Both hurdle wins have come at this track over a similar trip in 2024; sole win since came over fences but ran a solid race back over hurdles at Fairyhouse last time; blinkers are back on and has a chance up 1lb.
runners.comment: Solid run back over hurdles at Fairyhouse last time; blinkers back on; has a chance up 1lb

runners.name: Daydream Nation
runners.spotlight: Gowran maiden hurdle winner in October over 2m4f won a Leopardstown handicap over this trip in March and ran a fine third in a Listed Fairyhouse contest last time; up 2lb but should go well again with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Third in a Listed Fairyhouse race last time; up 2lb but go well again with cheekpieces on

runners.name: Bridie's Beau
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner up to 2m4f; hit and miss over fences during the winter (poor last twice) and has to return to best back over hurdles; 2lb out of the handicap
runners.comment: Poor chasing last twice; has to return to best back over hurdles; 2lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Grann's Boy
runners.spotlight: Three wins over hurdles up to 2m4f; decent Flat run in March but has to improve on his Fairyhouse hurdles run last time; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Has to improve on his Fairyhouse hurdles run last time; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Hewick
runners.spotlight: Has been a fantastic standard-bearer for his yard, winning 12 races between hurdles and fences including the King George in 2023; hasn't been at that level in the last year but ran respectably in the Stayers' Hurdle; has to bounce back from a poor Aintree run.
runners.comment: Ran respectably in the Stayers' Hurdle; has to bounce back from a poor Aintree run

runners.name: Ballybow
runners.spotlight: Won a Grade 3 hurdle over this trip in 2025 but hasn't repeated that form since; some fair runs in defeat over fences this season but has to get back to his best returned to hurdles; yard second-string.
runners.comment: Some fair runs in defeat over fences but has to get back to his best returned to hurdles

runners.name: Minella Sixo
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner was just denied in a Naas handicap two starts back; then ran a solid race in the Pertemps Final at Cheltenham; looks the yard's first-string but needs more.
runners.comment: Solid run in the Pertemps Final at Cheltenham; looks the yard first-string but needs more

runners.name: Duke Silver
runners.spotlight: Three hurdle wins and ran a solid race in the Pertemps Final at Cheltenham; however, was well below that level at Aintree since and has to bounce back.
runners.comment: Solid run in the Pertemps Final but was well below that level at Aintree; has to rebound

runners.name: Margaret's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Posted two chase wins in UK in the last two years but hasn't been at his best on last three starts; all hurdle runs came in France, with two wins in 2020; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: All hurdle runs came in France, with two wins in 2020; others make more appeal

runners.name: Champagne Chic
runners.spotlight: The last of his three wins in UK came in a Haydock Pertemps qualifier in February; ran respectably in the final of that series at Cheltenham but has to do more to score today.
runners.comment: Ran respectably in the Pertemps Final at Cheltenham but has to do more to score today

runners.name: The Big Clubman
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m maiden hurdle on rules debut at Wexford last October; was second in a 2m4f Grade 3 at Navan next time but disappointed in a 2m7f Limerick Grade 2 after; runner-up in a Newbury novice last time; cheekpieces added; this trip is a query.
runners.comment: Grade 3 runner-up was also 2nd in a Newbury novice last time; this trip is a query

runners.name: Chance Another One
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won over hurdles at Ballinrobe last summer but was disappointing when last seen in this sphere at Cheltenham last autumn when wore a hood; however, has improved plenty over fences since and ran a cracker in an Aintree Grade 3 last time; 4lb out of the handicap but hurdles mark is 14lb lower than chase one; check the market if getting a run.
runners.comment: 4lb out of h'cap but hurdles mark is 14lb lower than chase one; check the market; reserve

runners.name: Billy Lee Swagger
runners.spotlight: Won a Fairyhouse handicap in November but was poor twice since over hurdles and once over fences; little appeal from 2lb out of the handicap; blinkers.
runners.comment: Poor twice over hurdles and once over fences lately; little appeal 2lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Sign From Above
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner won his second hurdle race at Gowran over 2m4f last October but was pulled-up at this track next time; poor on the Flat latest; others make more appeal
runners.comment: Won at Gowran over 2m4f last October but was pulled-up at this track next time

runners.name: Karl Des Tourelles
runners.spotlight: Has only one win over hurdles but ran a cracker to be fourth in this race last year off the same mark; ran over fences without winning first four runs this season but a fine effort when not the clearest of passages in the Martin Pipe last time; a player.
runners.comment: Fourth in this last year; not the clearest of runs in the Martin Pipe last time; a player

runners.name: Icare Desbois
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper and maiden hurdle over 2m for Willie Mullins in an interrupted career; mixed form on the Flat and over hurdles for this yard; has to do much better raised markedly in trip.
runners.comment: Mixed form on the Flat and over hurdles; has to do much better raised markedly in trip

runners.name: Ballystone
runners.spotlight: Won for the second time over hurdles when taking a 3m Galway handicap last August; however, is 8lb higher today in a stronger race and has to be at his best on return.
runners.comment: Won a Galway handicap last August; 8lb higher today in a stronger race on return

runners.name: Da Capo Glory
runners.spotlight: Three hurdle wins including at this track and ran respectably in this race in 2024; mixing chasing with hurdles after returning from an absence this season; not a bad run in the Martin Pipe last time; needs a bit more.
runners.comment: Ran well in this in 2024;  not a bad run in the Martin Pipe last time; needs a bit more

races.race_name: Frontline Security Handicap Chase (Listed Race)

runners.name: Relieved Of Duties
runners.spotlight: Found things too demanding in the Grand Annual, and has been campaigned ambitiously overall since a Fairyhouse beginners' win last October; will be more at home in this company.
runners.comment: Found things too demanding in the Grand Annual, will be more at home in this company

runners.name: Petit Tonnerre
runners.spotlight: Out of form since his 16-1 win in this race last year; possibly back on a competitive mark now, only 3lb higher than 12 months ago; however, needs to step up on the form of his Aintree run.
runners.comment: Out of form since his 16-1 win in this race last year, back on a competitive mark

runners.name: Jalila Moriviere
runners.spotlight: A 135-rated hurdler trounced weak opposition on chase debut at Sligo last October; best chase run was over further in a Grade 3 contest at Thurles; never a threat behind today's rival Come Walk With Me at Fairyhouse on latest.
runners.comment: Best chase run was over further in a Grade 3, never a threat at Fairyhouse on latest

runners.name: Ballysax Hank
runners.spotlight: Summer Plate winner at Market Rasen (2m5f, good) last July and went close in the American Grand National (2m5f, firm) in October; excuses on both winter starts; has around 5lb to find with Release The Beast on Cheltenham running; big task under this weight and would prefer further.
runners.comment: Approx' 5l to find with Release The Beast on Cheltenham running, big ask under this weight

runners.name: Birdie Or Bust
runners.spotlight: Very useful hurdler, two wins and third in 2024 Cheltenham Mares' Novices Hurdle; took a while to find her form over fences but won at Killarney and Listowel last year; second in Grade 3 novice here in October; never counted at Fairyhouse after a break.
runners.comment: Never counted at Fairyhouse after a break, better judged on decent form last autumn

runners.name: Sky Lord
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler; yet to score over fences; beaten favourite at Cork in his first handicap chase; needs to raise his game in first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite at Cork in his first handicap chase, needs to raise his game, tongue-tie

runners.name: Seskin Flash
runners.spotlight: Improved for fitting of a hood, winning 2m6f Downpatrick handicap hurdle last season and produced late to land 2m4f beginners' chase at same venue in October; made the frame in both handicap attempts before a break; has a bit to find with Release The Beast on Leopardstown running; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in both handicap attempts since a beginners' win, worth considering

runners.name: McLaurey
runners.spotlight: Beat the talented Storm Heart in a valuable DRF handicap hurdle in 2024; maiden over fences; seventh when well fancied for a Cheltenham handicap; suffered interference off the home turn, might have finished closer; addition of tongue-tie may help.
runners.comment: Maiden over fences, unplaced when well fancied for a Cheltenham handicap, tongue-tie now

runners.name: Path d'Oroux
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 21 starts over fences, placed seven times; a long way below his best at Cork last time and well held by third-placed Sky Lord.
runners.comment: A long way below his best at Cork last time and well held by third-placed Sky Lord

runners.name: Golden Joy
runners.spotlight: Pulled up after making a bad mistake in the race in which Release The Beast was second at Leopardstown in December; stepped up from a fourth placing at Naas when second in a valuable DRF handicap; Western Diego fourth then, Ballysax Hank sixth; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Could feature if reproducing the form of his second at the DRF, Western Diego fourth then

runners.name: Escapeandevade
runners.spotlight: Bounced back from an off day at Sandown to win a small-field Newbury event; this is tougher, but he is generally reliable.
runners.comment: Bounced back from an off day at Sandown to win small-field Newbury event, this is tougher

runners.name: Dont Go Yet
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner who got off the mark over hurdles at Wexford in March; fair third back over fences at the same venue last time; held by several of these on running in a DRF handicap.
runners.comment: Veteran recorded a first hurdles win in March, fair third back over fences last time

runners.name: The King Of Prs
runners.spotlight: Won the Dan & Joan Moore at Fairyhouse (2m1f, soft) early last year; well held by Western Diego on running in the same race this year; uneven form over the last 15 months; fell three out in the Topham when 50-1 chance.
runners.comment: Fell three out in the Topham when 50-1 chance, well held by Western Diego on winter form

runners.name: Come Walk With Me
runners.spotlight: Failed to win over hurdles, second in three of his six starts; has developed into a better chaser; bolted up at Fairyhouse, form franked by the second Moudan in winning at Ayr; 11lb rise means that he has plenty to find in a renewal of Leopardstown rivalry with Raffles Dolce Vita.
runners.comment: Bolted up at Fairyhouse, plenty to find with Raffles Dolce Vita on Leopardstown running

runners.name: Release The Beast
runners.spotlight: Second-season chaser who showed improved form when runner-up at Wexford and Leopardstown on his first two starts this season; Grand Annual fourth confirmed a level of form that makes him interesting in this company; good 5lb claimer.
runners.comment: Strong candidate based on seconds at Wexford and Leopardstown and Grand Annual fourth

runners.name: Western Diego
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent and just one win from his nine chase starts but that came in the Grade 3 Dan & Joan Moore Memorial Handicap at Fairyhouse (2m1f, yielding) in January; weakened when fourth of 14 in a 2m1f DRF handicap, only 3l behind runner-up Golden Joy; Grand Annual faller.
runners.comment: Grand Annual faller, only 3l behind runner-up Golden Joy when fourth in valuable DRF race

runners.name: Coral River
runners.spotlight: Recent novice win was gained at 2m4f at Clonmel; however, worth bearing in mind that he also boasts useful form over this course and trip.
runners.comment: Recent novice win was gained at 2m4f but he also boasts useful form at around this trip

runners.name: Raffles Dolce Vita
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler in France; soundly beaten in first three chase starts for this trainer; left previous Irish form behind when beating Come Walk With Me at Leopardstown; youngest horse in the race at five, and probably the one with most potential for improvement.
runners.comment: Ex-French, left previous Irish form behind when beating Come Walk With Me at Leopardstown

runners.name: Tuckmill
runners.spotlight: Unseated at the first in this race last year; no sign of his best form for a long time; unplaced off his lower hurdles mark last time; 2lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Unseated at the first in this race last year, no sign of his best form for a long time

runners.name: Indiana Jones
runners.spotlight: Out of sorts for a long time before returning to form with a fine effort to finish fifth in the Topham; had decent 2m form early in his career
runners.comment: Returned to form with a fine effort in the Topham, had decent 2m form early in his career

races.race_name: Barberstown Castle Novice Chase (Grade 1)

runners.name: Kopek Des Bordes
runners.spotlight: Was the outstanding novice hurdler of last season with two Grade 1 wins including the Supreme Novices' at Cheltenham; had an excuse when well below that form at this meeting last year; made an impressive chasing debut at Navan; then had a chip taken out of a knee, so lacked experience but still ran a cracker in the Arkle; with more to come he will be difficult to beat today.
runners.comment: Ran a cracker in the Arkle; with more to come he will be difficult to beat today

runners.name: Jacob's Ladder
runners.spotlight: Grade 3 novice hurdle winner over 2m4f last season but is comfortable at this trip too; got off the mark over fences at Fairyhouse before winning a valuable Leopardstown handicap and Navan Grade 3, all over approx' 2m; fine fourth in the WillowWarm Gold Cup last time at 2m4f; can be involved.
runners.comment: Three chase wins incl' Grade 3; 4th in Grade 1 last time over 2m4f; can be involved

runners.name: Salvator Mundi
runners.spotlight: Won a Grade 2 novice hurdle at this track last season before adding a Grade 1 at Aintree; easily got off the mark over fences at Thurles on third start but was a beaten favourite in an Aintree Grade 1 last time when raced keenly; has a bit to find with stablemate Kopek Des Bordes but is capable of a big run.
runners.comment: Beaten fav' in an Aintree Grade 1 last time; bit to find with stablemate Kopek Des Bordes

runners.name: Irish Panther
runners.spotlight: Ran some fine races in defeat over hurdles last season including at this meeting; got off the mark in a Clonmel maiden last autumn and then took a Naas beginners' chase, easily beating Jacob's Ladder; just denied in the Grade 1 Racing Post Novice Chase at Christmas and put up a respectable effort in the Champion Chase last time; can be involved.
runners.comment: Just denied in the Grade 1 Racing Post Novice; fair run in Champion Chase; can be involved

runners.name: Pure Steel
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner was 12l behind Irish Panther on chasing debut at Naas; has been hit and miss since but both his wins in that time have been over C&D so his course form can help him back here; has to rebound from a bad mistake at Fairyhouse last time.
runners.comment: Both chase wins have been over C&D; has to rebound from a bad mistake at Fairyhouse

races.race_name: Ladbrokes Champion Stayers Hurdle (Grade 1)

runners.name: Teahupoo
runners.spotlight: Stayers' Hurdle winner at Cheltenham in 2024; second to Bob Olinger last year; experienced a rare off day when bidding to regain his crown last month, lost place at eighth and ran into traffic when rallying; despite that, strong chance of winning this race for the third time and adding to this season's Grade 1 wins at Fairyhouse and Leopardstown; blinkered for the first time.
runners.comment: Had a rare off day at Cheltenham, blinkered now in a bid to win this race for a third time

runners.name: Honesty Policy
runners.spotlight: Won the Grade 1 Mersey Novices' Hurdle last year (2m4f, good); ended the season with second over C&D; all three runs this season in Grade 1s, third in the Long Walk at Ascot (3m, good to soft), fifth in the Stayers' Hurdle at Cheltenham (3m, good), third behind Home By The Lee at Aintree; could be in the mix for Teahupoo's trainer.
runners.comment: Smart form in defeat in Grade 1 staying races, talented second-string for Elliott yard

runners.name: Bob Olinger
runners.spotlight: Ended a sequence of seconds when beating Teahupoo in the 2025 Stayers' Hurdle; no match for Teahupoo at Leopardstown on seasonal debut; had that below-par rival behind when a fine third behind Home By The Lee in the Stayers'; continues to prosper despite his veteran status; can go close.
runners.comment: Beat Teahupoo in 2025 Stayers', form reversed on return, came out best again at Cheltenham

runners.name: Jimmy Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: Ran only once last season, winning the Coral Cup at Cheltenham when returning from a long absence; four races over fences this season; jumping has let him down since starting with an emphatic Fairyhouse win; made a mistake two out in 2m4f Grade 1 at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Coral Cup winner, chasing this season, let down by his jumping after Fairyhouse win

runners.name: Kawaboomga
runners.spotlight: Smart form as a novice last season, despite running only twice; off for over a year before finishing fourth in the 2m Red Mills at Gowran in February; reversed form of that race with stablemate Storm Heart when second to a rejuvenated Slade Steel in 2m4f Grade 2 at Fairyhouse; unknown quantity at this trip.
runners.comment: Second to Slade Steel in 2m4f Grade 2 at Fairyhouse, unknown quantity at this trip.

runners.name: Jetara
runners.spotlight: Third in this event last year after taking the same place in Aintree Grade 1; has failed to match that form this season and has slumped 9lb in the ratings in 12 months; tailed off in Leopardstown Grade 1 won by Teahupoo; moderate fifth behind Wodhooh; a lot to prove now but could surprise.
runners.comment: Third in this event last year, nothing like the same standard of form since then

runners.name: Franciscan Rock
runners.spotlight: Most recent of his three wins was in 2024; ran his best race for a long time when 50-1 runner-up in the BetMGM Cup at Cheltenham; remote eighth behind Teahupoo here 12 months ago; up against it.
runners.comment: 50-1 runner-up in Cheltenham handicap, remote eighth behind Teahupoo here 12 months ago

runners.name: Meet And Greet
runners.spotlight: Has been chasing since pulled up in this race last year; 66-1 chance then; an unenviable task again; first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Has been chasing since pulled up in this race last year, faces an unenviable task again

races.race_name: JP & M Doyle (C & G) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Look Me
runners.spotlight: Went close for Stuart Crawford on debut in a valuable sales bumper at the Fairyhouse Easter meeting last year; made a winning start for Willie Mullins at the same venue in February; proved his effectiveness on less testing ground when keeping on for second behind the promising Forthfactor in a 20-runner Grade 2 bumper at Aintree; smart second-string for stable.
runners.comment: Second to a promising type in Grade 2 bumper at Aintree, smart second-string for stable

runners.name: Bon Bon Fizz
runners.spotlight: Beaten when falling at the last in sole point-to-point start; sent off 100-1 when an eyecatcher in a Thurles maiden hurdle in February, keeping on well and 5l off the leader when falling at the last; made amends with a wide-margin bumper win at the same venue a month later; has left Paul O'Flynn to join Gordon Elliott in the Gigginstown colours.
runners.comment: Promising Thurles winner has left Paul O'Flynn to join Gordon Elliott for Gigginstown

runners.name: Our Trigger
runners.spotlight: Brother to Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Gaelic Warrior; not much pace in the four-runner contest in which he landed the odds in testing conditions at Gowran in January; not among the stable's main fancies for the Champion Bumper, disappointingly tame effort nonetheless.
runners.comment: Brother to Gaelic Warrior, beat his three rivals at Gowran on debut, in rear at Cheltenham

runners.name: Soul Asylum
runners.spotlight: Narrowly failed to win on debut for Colin Bowe in a maiden at Dromahane last November, losing out to Red Acres Max who has since won a small-field bumper at Carlisle for Jamie Snowden; fetched £280,000 at the sales (£40,000 more than the winner); difficult to know what he achieved in winning a slowly-run four-runner C&D event in February.
runners.comment: Hard to know what he achieved in winning a slowly-run four-runner C&D event in February

runners.name: Quiryn
runners.spotlight: Travelled powerfully to win first time out at Naas in January; proved best of the Mullins squad at Cheltenham, finishing one place in front of The Wager who took his chance in Wednesday's Grade 1; needs to settle better (was very keen for Paul Townend) but seems to have plenty of ability.
runners.comment: Stylish winner on debut at Naas, promising at Cheltenham despite racing very keenly

runners.name: Outofafrika
runners.spotlight: Won his sole point start for Tom Lacey last year; not particularly strong in the market when making a satisfactory debut for this yard at Leopardstown at Christmas, beaten 10l in fifth behind subsequent Cheltenham winner The Mourne Rambler; showed the benefit of the run when much too strong for a Willie Mullins odds-on shot at Fairyhouse in February.
runners.comment: Produced a bright display to beat a Willie Mullins odds-on shot at Fairyhouse in February

runners.name: It's Good To Talk
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to five winners, notably smart Flat performer Raise You and Champion Bumper winner A Dream To Share; chased home impressive 10l winner Low Kick (Grade 1 runner on Wednesday) at Naas in February.
runners.comment: Chased home impressive winner Low Kick (Grade 1 runner on Wednesday) at Naas in February

runners.name: Cowboy Casanova
runners.spotlight: Promising third in a point for Colin Bowe last November; made a good start under rules when second of 15 behind an impressive winner at Newcastle in January; had to settle for third at Uttoxeter last month; faces a stiff task against Irish bumper winners.
runners.comment: Placed in a point and in two bumpers in Britain, stiff task against Irish bumper winners

runners.name: Bois d'Angos
runners.spotlight: Under pressure when exiting three out in his only point-to-point start last season; betrayed his inexperience when around 12l third of four behind today's rival Look Me at Fairyhouse in February; still looked raw at Limerick last month and did well in the circumstances to beat the odds-on Border Lad.
runners.comment: Still looked raw when winning at Limerick, will need to raise his game in this company

runners.name: Adaboy Mushy
runners.spotlight: Blue Bresil gelding made all to justify favouritism in an eight-runner Leopardstown contest at the beginning of March; left an impression that he could find plenty of improvement; hooded now.
runners.comment: Made all to justify favouritism in eight-runner Leopardstown contest, open to improvement

runners.name: Highlander Addict
runners.spotlight: Decent pedigree; made debut at Galway last July, stayed on in the closing stages after hanging off the final bend, finished seventh of 19; took a step in the right direction when second at Navan in September, though beaten 11l; a lot more needed.
runners.comment: Took a step in the right direction when second at Navan last September, a lot more needed

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Handicap

runners.name: Shielas Well
runners.spotlight: With Ian Williams for last two outings, finding 7f too sharp on first occasion and maybe 1m3f too far on second; now she splits the difference and is very well handicapped judged on plenty of form, including a 1m2f win last June; could be dangerous in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Third run for yard; very well handicapped on plenty of form and has first-time headgear

runners.name: Dash Power
runners.spotlight: Gained sole win (44 races) in a classified in January 2025; eight seconds too, including two on AW this year; changed hands and left Lawrence Mullaney since latest start and that change of scenery needs to spark something.
runners.comment: Does not look the percentage call unless the change of trainers sparks something

runners.name: Hibernate
runners.spotlight: Frequent front-runner who won by a short head off this mark during a solid sequence over 1m2f last summer/autumn, including under Mason Paetel; back-to-form second of five at Leicester on latest outing, so big shout.
runners.comment: Back-to-form second of five at Leicester on latest outing, so has a big shout

runners.name: Prince Hector
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent and all three career wins were on AW, the latest last March; finished second or third in seven of his last 13 starts but rarely seen on turf these days; tongue tied first time.
runners.comment: All three wins on AW, latest last March; rarely seen on turf these days; first tongue-tie

runners.name: Merapi
runners.spotlight: 0-9 last year for Ed Walker; new yard this year and her half-length second of 14 here (good) last time was her closest finish, over 1m but she ran on well from rear; 1m1f, at the least, will be within range on that evidence.
runners.comment: Half-length second here (1m) last time was her closest finish, running on well from rear

runners.name: Duke Orsino
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden, never always finishing towards rear; tried cheekpieces on latest start; now it's a step up in trip.
runners.comment: 0-11, never always finishing towards rear; cheekpieces latest; now it's a step up in trip

runners.name: Twilight Guest
runners.spotlight: 1m2f turf hat-trick last May/June (Brighton and Pontefract); went off the boil and last of five on reappearance in January but he's well handicapped if springing back to life; 0-15 record at Yarmouth includes some places.
runners.comment: 1m2f turf hat-trick last May/June and well handicapped if springing back to life

races.race_name: Branfords Restaurant & Bar Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Baandee
runners.spotlight: Second in her three starts, all over 7f at Chelmsford (AW) and from the front on first two occasions; should play a major role if okay on turf.
runners.comment: 2nd in her three starts, all 7f at Chelmsford (AW); from the front on first two occasions

runners.name: Fashion People
runners.spotlight: Mehmas first foal of a 7f 2yo winner (RPR 88); 1-2 for her first appearance in a six-runner maiden at Newcastle (7f, AW) in November and she led over 1f out for a clearcut win; promising.
runners.comment: 1-2 at Newcastle (7f, AW; six ran) in November and she led over 1f out for a clearcut win

runners.name: Venetian Sky
runners.spotlight: 300,000euros yearling by St Mark's Basilica; tenth of 11 when 33-1 for a novice at Newmarket (7f, good to soft) in November but she showed promise to be in touch for 4f and could be the type to do a lot better.
runners.comment: Tenth of 11 when 33-1 at Newmarket (7f, good to soft) in November but she showed promise

runners.name: Me An' All
runners.spotlight: 150-1, beaten 18l in a newcomers race at Newmarket (1m, good) two weeks ago; should improve but hard to fancy just yet.
runners.comment: 150-1, beaten 18l in a newcomers race at Newmarket (1m, good) two weeks ago

runners.name: Too Too Divine
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling by Starman; weak 12-1 for maiden at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in November, chasing the all-the-way winner over 4f before fading into last of four (all from major stables); could be a big improver this season, which she needs to be; market may guide.
runners.comment: Last of four in one AW race; could be a big improver this season, which she needs to be

runners.name: Night Shining
runners.spotlight: 475,000gns yearling by Dark Angel; 9-2, slowly away and never dangerous when about 8l sixth of eight in maiden at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last July; that's all that was seen of her as a 2yo but she did show some promise.
runners.comment: 475,000gns buy; 9-2, sixth of 8 at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last July but some promise

races.race_name: Gold & Silver Exchange Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Alpine Culture
runners.spotlight: Improved when third of eight in maiden at Newmarket (7f, good; 10-1) 16 days ago, always prominent and just ahead of Kimbara; that's probably the best form in this line-up and it looks as if 1m will help.
runners.comment: Third at Newmarket (7f, good; 10-1) 16 days ago is probably the best form and 1m can help

runners.name: Fahidi
runners.spotlight: Some market support when showing fair form in a maiden at Sandown (7f, good) last July and novice at Haydock (1m, good) in September; carries head high but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Carries head high but still enters calculations after showing fair form on both 2yo starts

runners.name: Euston Hall
runners.spotlight: Showed promise at Kempton in November before his second of eight in novice at Newcastle (7f, AW) one month later; no match for the winner (useful since) in the latter but ran on well and shaped encouragingly.
runners.comment: Second of eight at Newcastle (7f, AW) on second 2yo start, shaping encouragingly

runners.name: Kimbara
runners.spotlight: Only fourth of five at Newcastle (6f, AW) on debut in March but much better when about 3l fourth of eight in maiden at Newmarket (7f, good; 50-1) 16 days ago, just over 1l behind Alpine Culture; that puts him in the mix and he can probably improve again.
runners.comment: About 3l fourth of 8 at Newmarket (7f, good; 50-1) 16 days ago, just behind Alpine Culture

runners.name: Venetian Lion
runners.spotlight: By St Mark's Basilica; tenth foal; closely related to Group-placed 7.5f AW 2yo winner Golden Crown (RPR 104), half-brother to seven winners including Forest Of Dean (1m2f, including Group 3; 116), English Oak (7f/1m; 114) and Crown Of Oaks (1m/1m2f; 107); dam 6f/1m winner (98); withdrawn with self certificates in September and 12 days ago; newcomer to note.
runners.comment: Tenth foal; the dam's eight winners include seven with top RPRs of 101-116; one to note

runners.name: Adamlyi
runners.spotlight: Kingman half-brother to Mujtaba (Group-placed 7.5f-1m2f; RPR 118); gambled-on co-favourite for maiden at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last September but ended up fifth of eight, running green; very early days and it will be interesting to see if his backers return.
runners.comment: Gambled on at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last September but ended up fifth of eight

runners.name: That Darn Cantor
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and 125-1 when always towards rear at Newmarket (1m, good) in October and Nottingham (1m2f, good) three weeks ago, finishing last.
runners.comment: 40-1 and 125-1 when last at Newmarket (1m) in October and Nottingham (1m2f) recently

runners.name: Ziggy's Avenger
runners.spotlight: Beat only one rival at Nottingham (8.5f, soft; 28-1) in October and two at Southwell (1m, AW; 100-1) 15 days ago.
runners.comment: Behind at Nottingham (8.5f, soft; 28-1) in October and Southwell (1m; 100-1) 15 days ago
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing & Harry Kassap Llc

runners.name: Revelio
runners.spotlight: Dam 7f 2yo/1m Listed winner who was runner-up in 1,000 Guineas; 18-1 from 12-1, faded into sixth of ten in maiden at Leicester (1m2f, good) last April; left Richard Hannon for 25,000gns in July, before gelding and wind operations early this year; work to do.
runners.comment: Leicester 6th (1m2f) last April; left R Hannon for 25,000gns in July; gelding and wind op

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap

runners.name: Alice De Clare
runners.spotlight: Looked an interesting handicap debutante at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) last Thursday and so it proved, as she made a big move from rear to lead over 1f out but was beaten a neck; due to go up 7lb after this, so has a good chance if she's in the same form on this quick return to action.
runners.comment: Big move to take lead at Beverley (1m2f) last Thursday but beaten a neck; due to go up 7lb

runners.name: Time To Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Saddle slipped three starts ago but she made amends with a short-head win at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in March; not discredited in fifth of eight here (1m, good) 19 days ago but she raced too freely and didn't shape as well as third-placed Sports Day.
runners.comment: Short-head win at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW); didn't shape as well as Sports Day here latest

runners.name: Heart Sign
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on second or third starts but he's not made a serious impact since in his two handicaps (probably stayed 1m2f last time); this is his first turf race since debut.
runners.comment: Has not made a serious impact in his two handicaps, though probably stayed 1m2f last time

runners.name: Sports Day
runners.spotlight: Gelded after 2yo campaign and improved when a close, always-prominent third of eight here (1m, good) on handicap/seasonal debut 19 days ago, shaping as if 1m2f may well help; major player.
runners.comment: Close third here (1m, good) on handicap/seasonal debut, shaping as if 1m2f may well help

runners.name: Roserunner
runners.spotlight: Won a Lingfield novice (1m, AW) in December on second start and narrowly beaten on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in January; however, this turf debut with first-time cheekpieces requires him to bounce back from a lesser show in late January.
runners.comment: AW win; turf debut with cheekpieces, needing to bounce back from a modest show in January

runners.name: King's Courtier
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three starts last year (6f-1m) but much better when fourth of eight when 66-1 in maiden at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) three weeks ago; needs to build on that on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Better in AW maiden (9.4f) three weeks ago; needs to build on that on this handicap debut

runners.name: Grindleton
runners.spotlight: Not far away at Pontefract or Bath (again 1m on good to soft) last autumn on his second and third starts; makes his handicap debut and some improvement should be forthcoming this season.
runners.comment: Makes his handicap debut and some improvement should be forthcoming this season

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Beach Point
runners.spotlight: Won at Sandown (1m2f) last summer in first two starts after cheekpieces were refitted; respectable in two of four starts since, including over C&D recently when finishing on the heels of A Major Payne; should be competitive.
runners.comment: C&D recently saw him finish on the heels of A Major Payne; should be competitive
runners.owner: John & Peter Seabrook And Tim Fisher

runners.name: A Major Payne
runners.spotlight: 0-15 but involved in some tight finishes at 1m and also over C&D recently on his second go at 1m2f (one place ahead of Beach Point); off the same mark, he should be seriously involved if the returning cheekpieces (worn on penultimate start last term, ran creditably) don't have a negative effect.
runners.comment: 0-15 but in some tight finishes at 1m and another over C&D recently on second go at 1m2f

runners.name: Golden Horse
runners.spotlight: 425,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; fair form last July-September on all three starts, though apparently best when off the mark in a weak race at Hamilton (1m, good; made all) on second occasion; well beaten in a 1m2f Newmarket novice on final outing; after only three races, this handicap debutant could still be capable of better but it is needed.
runners.comment: After three races (one win) this handicap debutant could still do better but it is needed

runners.name: Lordsbridge Blu
runners.spotlight: 1-15 in handicaps, with the win on Lingfield AW (AW) last November but he's often been thereabouts, including on turf; bang there on Wolverhampton AW on both outings this term, the latest just three days ago; player.
runners.comment: 1-15 in handicaps but often thereabouts, including on turf and AW just three days ago

runners.name: Clouds Hill
runners.spotlight: Second on turf last April (good) and June (good to firm) before a short-priced AW win at Newcastle in November, all over 1m; while not discredited in form terms on handicap debut at Kempton (1m, AW; 10-1) in December, he managed only seventh of 11 and showed that overall improvement is needed; lightly raced and 1m2f should be within reach.
runners.comment: Needs overall improvement in his second handicap; lightly raced and 1m2f should be okay
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

races.race_name: Sky Sports Virgin 512 Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Second at 2-5 and evens after catching the eye on Newbury debut (finished third) but she made no mistake in a four-runner novice at Kempton (6f, AW) six weeks ago and the odds-on runner-up won next time; return to 7f looks in order and, although this handicap debut is more of a test, she seems to have a competitive mark.
runners.comment: Return to 7f looks in order and she seems to have a competitive mark for handicap debut

runners.name: Zighy
runners.spotlight: Steadily going the right way, on last two starts winning in a novice at Wolverhampton in November and handicap debut at Southwell 15 days ago; both of those were 6f on AW under good claimer Jack Callan; up 6lb but strength at the finish last time makes 7f today an appealing option.
runners.comment: Steady progress on AW; strength at finish last time makes this 7f an appealing option
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Angels' Share
runners.spotlight: 7f on Lingfield AW for her quick double late last summer in a maiden and nursery, both from the front; did those in some style but found a hot fillies' race at Goodwood (7f, good to soft) in September far too demanding; player if back to her best for a yard that seems to be in good form.
runners.comment: 7f on Lingfield AW for quick double last summer; player if back to best for in-form yard

runners.name: Wetsand
runners.spotlight: In good form in her early starts on turf last year but soundly beaten facing much stiffer tasks on next two outings; remained out of form this season in 10.5f Listed race at Saint-Cloud (heavy) and 7f handicap at Southwell (AW) and that's a major worry.
runners.comment: Out of form in 10.5f Listed race at Saint-Cloud (heavy) and 7f handicap at Southwell (AW)

runners.name: You'll Think Of Me
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in four runs for Donnacha O'Brien in Ireland but she showed progressive form for him and made a winning debut for new yard in five-runner novice at Kempton (6f, AW) in December; the latter was at 1-3, admittedly, but she stays 7f and goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Progressive Irish form before off the mark when 1-3 at Kempton (6f, AW) in December

runners.name: Brisk Symphony
runners.spotlight: 6f maiden and novice winner on good to firm last June/July; ran further good races when fourth of 12 in a Doncaster nursery (6.5f, good to soft) and eighth in a Newmarket Listed race (6f, good); better is needed over this new trip on return but her stable is in fine form, including with two recent 3yo reappearance winners.
runners.comment: Better is needed over this new trip on return but her stable is in fine form

runners.name: Here I Bow
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam lightly raced, placed in Germany at 1m1f, out of unraced half-sister to German 1m Group 2 and US 1m2f Grade 2 winner Sumitas; 7f maiden win last June from seven starts in France for Florian Guyander; peak efforts on softer than good; marked improvement is seemingly needed on British debut; rookie trainer's reappearance winner this month was heavily backed.
runners.comment: 7f maiden winner last June from seven starts in France; peak efforts on softer than good

runners.name: Harlequin Angel
runners.spotlight: Outclassed in a valuable sales race in October but progressive going into that, culminating in a front-running win on handicap debut at Newmarket (7f, good to firm); 5lb higher for this return and 7lb claimer is recruited.
runners.comment: Outclassed in valuable sales race in October but progressive going into that; 7lb claimer

races.race_name: Eastern Power Systems 'Confined' Handicap (For horses Which Have Won No More Than One Race)

runners.name: Del Corso
runners.spotlight: Useful pedigree and he showed improved form when a close third of 12 on seasonal/handicap debut at Nottingham (1m, good) three weeks ago, favourite and mounting a strong challenge in the final 1f; up 3lb but strong chance.
runners.comment: Improved form when a close third of 12 on seasonal/handicap debut at Nottingham (1m, good)

runners.name: Armstrong
runners.spotlight: Back from seven months off to be second in handicaps at Kempton (1m, AW; strong finish) and Bath (1m, good to soft); that's three seconds from his five starts and he hung left last time but no shock if he now goes one better.
runners.comment: Second as 3yo in handicaps at Kempton (1m, AW; strong finish) and Bath (1m, good to soft)

runners.name: Okiru
runners.spotlight: 0-7, all over 7f on AW; sent handicapping on his last three starts; ran well in the first two of those before a lesser show in second-time cheekpieces on latest; 7lb claimer is enlisted for this turf debut with in-form yard.
runners.comment: 0-7, all 7f on AW; sent handicapping on his last three starts; ran well in the first two

runners.name: Zooter
runners.spotlight: Disappointing at 5-4 for handicap debut (1m, good) to end last season and reappearance in Wolverhampton maiden (9.4f, AW) wasn't his best form either, albeit providing the most resistance to a 1-2 favourite and 5l clear of the rest; return to 1m might help and cheekpieces are enlisted.
runners.comment: Chased home 1-2 shot in maiden latest; return to 1m may help and cheekpieces are enlisted

runners.name: Guest House
runners.spotlight: Gelded after third start last season and took third of nine on handicap debut here (7f, good) in October on his only appearance since; showed promise too, from off the pace, and isn't entirely ruled out despite the need for further progress.
runners.comment: Promise from off the pace when third of nine on handicap debut here (7f, good) in October

runners.name: Norfolk Blue
runners.spotlight: Second from off the pace in a novice at Lingfield (7f, good to soft) last July on second start but that stands out a mile from his other four performances; stamina on the dam's side; gelded prior to this reappearance for an in-form yard.
runners.comment: Novice 2nd at Lingfield (7f, good to soft) in July stands out from his other four efforts

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On X Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: El Lambo
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to three useful sprinters and made quite a promising debut when sixth of nine in what was probably a much better race than this at Newbury (5f, good) a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Quite promising sixth of nine in much better race than this at Newbury (5f)
runners.owner: The Lamb Inn Wantage Racing Team

runners.name: Dakota Brave
runners.spotlight: 19,000euros yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to winners Misty Grey (6f-7f including 2yo/AW; RPR 115), The Trader (7f-1m5f including 2yo, 101) and Chinese Jade (7f-1m1f including GB 2yo/HK; 90); dam 7f-1m2f winner (including 2yo/Group 1; 116) out of 1m3f Group 3 winner; with that pedigree, he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Out of a 1m2f Group 1 winner who has produced 2yo winners; one to consider

runners.name: Potters Liberty
runners.spotlight: 13,000euros yearling; fourth foal; brother to 5f/6f winner Rock N Roll Rocket (including 2yo/AW; RPR 77); dam French 5f 2yo/5.5f winner (89); stable not prolific with this age group.
runners.comment: Out of a French 2yo sprint winner; stable not prolific with this age group

runners.name: Seathegulls
runners.spotlight: Represents stable with modest strike-rate in 2yo races but just 5-1 and showed some early speed before weakening into midfield in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) last month.
runners.comment: Just 5-1 and showed some early speed before weakening into midfield in the Brocklesby

runners.name: Freedom Flame
runners.spotlight: £40,000 yearling; eighth foal; half-sister to winners Prince Of The Dark (5.7f 2yo; RPR 102), Crimson Spirit (7f including 2yo; 96), Simplify (5f/6f including AW 2yo; 88), Crimson Angel (5f AW; 86) and Shimla Rolaan (7f/1m including AW; 69); dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo; 92); the only filly in the line-up; makes appeal on paper.
runners.comment: Out of a fairly useful 5f/6f winner; the only filly in the line-up; appeals on paper

runners.name: Adoramus
runners.spotlight: 60,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to 6f winner Mukaafah (including 2yo AW; RPR 100); dam French 6f 2yo winner (93); has the right type of pedigree; market support would bring him into the equation.
runners.comment: Has the right type of pedigree; market support would bring him into the equation
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs R Everist And Partner

runners.name: Alpe d'Huez
runners.spotlight: 58,000euros yearling; third foal; half-brother to 1m5f winner Vorfreude (RPR 93); dam ran twice (42), half-sister to three winners over 1m+, out of 6f 2yo Group 3 winner; yard's 2yos already off to good start this season.
runners.comment: 58,000euros yearling; stable's 2yos already off to a good start this season

runners.name: Prince Kameo
runners.spotlight: 48,000gns yearling; second foal; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 96), half-sister to winners Oklahoma City (Group-placed 6f/7f 2yo), Neenee's Choice (1m/1m2f) and Cogital (1m4f-2m/hurdle); very rare 2yo runner at this time of year for the stable.
runners.comment: Out of a useful 1m2f winner; very rare 2yo runner at this time of year for the stable

runners.name: Greek Street
runners.spotlight: Out of a French 7f 2yo winner; only 5-1 for five-runner Nottingham novice (5f, good) three weeks ago but struggled to get competitive; needs to have learned a lot from that outing.
runners.comment: Only 5-1 for 5f Nottingham novice three weeks ago but struggled to get competitive
runners.owner: C Booth, P Boyle, S Coleby & S Smith

races.race_name: 13th Barry Gurr Memorial Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Top Star
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but gave good account when second in 6f Wolverhampton classified last month and his recent defeat over 7f there is not viewed harshly; swaps hood for first-time cheekpieces; could have a say.
runners.comment: 0-11 but ran well in Wolverhampton classified last month; headgear switched today

runners.name: Horus
runners.spotlight: Faded out of contention on last month's stable debut at Kempton (6f; first-time visor) but is well handicapped on his winter form for Mike Murphy and this C&D suits; sports refitted cheekpieces; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Faded out of contention on stable debut in March but well handicapped on winter form

runners.name: Poetic Jack
runners.spotlight: Has modest 2-33 strike-rate but one of the wins was gained over C&D and he returns here after two fairly good fourths, the latter in first-time visor at Southwell (5f) a fortnight ago; more appealing than most.
runners.comment: Has modest strike-rate but has won over C&D and returns here after two decent efforts

runners.name: Haziym
runners.spotlight: Habitual slow starter who is 0-24 in Britain; now 5lb lower than when finishing well for third at Kempton (6f) in December but this year's form has been disappointing; others are much less risky.
runners.comment: Habitual slow starter who is 0-24 in Britain; others are much less risky

runners.name: Elvetham
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with win at Kempton (6f) last month but two lesser efforts on Tapeta have followed and he's drawn wide here; needs help from the first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Won last month, after a break, but two lesser efforts followed; blinkers now added

runners.name: Southbank
runners.spotlight: Scored at Kempton (6f) in the autumn and made the frame four consecutive times (three times over C&D) in January/February; likely contender again on return from two-month break.
runners.comment: In the frame four consecutive times on Polytrack in early 2026; back from two-month break

runners.name: Manhattan Chute
runners.spotlight: Now 7lb lower than when causing 50-1 shock over 5f here in January but has struggled to keep up on all four starts since (5f-6f); cheekpieces refitted in bid to perk him up.
runners.comment: Has struggled on all four starts since shock 5f win here in January
runners.owner: J May, R L Field, W Trenchard, J Pook

runners.name: Molly Marine
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only five starts but safely held in her first two handicaps (7f/8.3f) and reverts to sprinting with quite a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Safely held in both handicaps (7f/8.3f); reverts to sprinting with quite a bit to prove

runners.name: Little Miss Magic
runners.spotlight: Seemed to respond well to first-time visor when keeping-on third at Chelmsford (6f) in January but safely held on both starts in February and her tendency to start slowly is always a worry.
runners.comment: Placed in first-time visor in January but safely held on both starts in February

runners.name: Walking On Clouds
runners.spotlight: Down the field on first four starts for Chelsea Banham but fared better when fifth in a Wolverhampton classified (6f; heavily backed) last month; needs to build on that effort but it will be interesting to see whether he's strong in the betting again.
runners.comment: Fared better when fifth last month (heavily backed); market likely to guide again

runners.name: Bluesy Moon
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden who has already had plenty of chances off basement marks; makes very limited appeal on first start since October; refitted cheekpieces replace visor.
runners.comment: 16-race maiden who has already had plenty of chances off basement marks

races.race_name: 13th Barry Gurr Memorial Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Fai Fai
runners.spotlight: Pretty consistent last spring-autumn but doesn't win very often and made a low-key reappearance over C&D last month; needs to have taken significant benefit from that recent outing.
runners.comment: Pretty consistent last year but doesn't often win and made a low-key return last month

runners.name: Porfin
runners.spotlight: 11 wins, the latest off basement mark over C&D in January; largely consistent since and may benefit from this drop back in trip, having raced too freely when fourth over 7f here on latest outing; back from two-month break.
runners.comment: Largely consistent since C&D win off basement mark in January; back from two-month break

runners.name: Mintana
runners.spotlight: Just held on to win 7f classified here in February but found things a bit tougher in her two subsequent handicaps (7f/6f); usual cheekpieces (left off last time) are refitted; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Narrow winner of 7f classified here in January; held in two subsequent handicaps (7f/6f)

runners.name: Big Time Rascal
runners.spotlight: Last season's campaign fizzled out but he made a fairly encouraging return in 6f Wolverhampton classified last month, keeping on steadily from the rear to finish in midfield; now 6lb lower than for last spring's win at Brighton (6f, good); a possible.
runners.comment: Made a fairly encouraging reappearance last month and remains on a good mark

runners.name: Daley T
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over 6f (handicap/classified) from seven starts since returning in December from a long absence but he's 0-22 overall and has outside draw.
runners.comment: Placed twice over 6f this year but has wide draw today and strike-rate stands at 0-22

runners.name: Mighty Ruler
runners.spotlight: Opened account with hard-fought 7f course win off today's mark in January but yet to make any further progress and was unable to land a telling blow when dropped back to 6f at Kempton last month.
runners.comment: Gained hard-fought 7f course win in January but yet to make any further progress

runners.name: Lion Ring
runners.spotlight: Two wins this year, both in Wolverhampton classified races (5f/6f); probably needs to find something extra in this handicap, though, and below form on last two starts (both 5f).
runners.comment: Winner of two Wolverhampton classifieds this year; probably needs to find extra here

runners.name: Alashos
runners.spotlight: Didn't fire over 7f here last month and is now 0-9 for Chris Gordon but keeping-on fourth of 12 at Kempton (6f) two starts ago was a pretty good effort and he remains on a good mark; considered, despite awkward draw.
runners.comment: Didn't fire over 7f here last month but earlier 6f form gives him a shout

runners.name: Battle Point
runners.spotlight: 1-48, the win gained over 5f here in 2023; back from a break with three pretty good efforts this year, the latest a 66-1 fourth at Kempton (6f), but still makes only limited appeal.
runners.comment: Ran okay on all three outings this year but has poor strike-rate and makes limited appeal

runners.name: Comedian Leader
runners.spotlight: Better known as a 7f/1m performer but her latest win (last August) was gained over 6f; predictably found 5f too sharp here last month but finished well and wasn't beaten far; back up in trip today and well handicapped; interesting.
runners.comment: Predictably found 5f too sharp last time but finished well; remains on a good mark

runners.name: Harry Brown
runners.spotlight: Four-time C&D winner, most recently off 3lb higher in October; absent since two below-par runs in February and not one to rely upon heavily but still features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Below form on both starts in February but this four-time C&D winner is back on a good mark
runners.owner: Peter Crate And Gallagher Bloodstock Ltd

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap

runners.name: Tuscan Point
runners.spotlight: Won easily in today's headgear combination on last month's seasonal/stable debut at Southwell (6f, AW) and ran big race in defeat when dropped back to 5f at Catterick (good; wore cheekpieces) last week; quickly back in action before latest 2lb rise takes effect and may still have more to offer for new trainer.
runners.comment: Won on last month's stable debut; 2lb well in after clear second at Catterick last week

runners.name: Wedgewood
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who capitalised upon good mark when winning from stall 1 over C&D six weeks ago; only 2lb higher today but this race is competitive and others appeal more.
runners.comment: C&D winner off reduced mark last month; only 2lb higher today but this race is competitive

runners.name: Desdemona
runners.spotlight: Dual 5f Polytrack winner (once here) this year; not discredited in higher-grade company over 6f here last month and probably still on a workable mark for this drop back to her optimum trip; must be considered.
runners.comment: Dual 5f Polytrack winner this year; back over optimum trip after respectable 6f run

runners.name: She Went Whoosh
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly when fourth over C&D last week but is now 0-11 for Scott Dixon and her overall profile remains unappealing.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly when fourth over C&D last week but is now 0-11 for Scott Dixon

runners.name: Honour Your Dreams
runners.spotlight: On the downgrade now but placed over C&D on two of his last three appearances; no match for Desdemona when second on latter occasion but now 11lb better off; can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: On downgrade now but placed over C&D on two of last three starts; can make presence felt

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Ammoony
runners.spotlight: Appeared to show improvement when close second of four behind odds-on favourite on seasonal debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last month, albeit with the form of that steadily run race quite tricky to assess; upped in trip for this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Improved form when close second in small-field novice (8.6f) last month; handicap debut

runners.name: Bergamo Gold
runners.spotlight: Came good with two wins (one over C&D) in March; still in good form but was allowed to set steady fractions when making the frame on her last two appearances (9.4f/C&D) and others in this field may have more potential.
runners.comment: Won twice in March (once over C&D); still in good form but others may have more potential

runners.name: Flightofthephoenix
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after ten starts but she's well handicapped on some of last year's placed form and endured a wide trip when only seventh over C&D in January on latest outing; contender if tuned up after break.
runners.comment: Absent since January but well handicapped on some of last year's placed form
runners.owner: Old Gold Racing, Tim Cottier & Partner

runners.name: Guendolen
runners.spotlight: Absent since two below-par turf runs in September but an emphatic winner when fresh last April and begins her new campaign from a workable mark; placed on only AW start (in Tapeta in 2024); no surprise if she features.
runners.comment: Off since two below-par runs in September but an emphatic winner when fresh last April

runners.name: Gnocchi
runners.spotlight: Ran as though something was amiss on nursery debut in the autumn (6f) but last month's reappearance at Kempton (1m) was not devoid of promise; upped in trip again here; not out of this if that recent run brought her on.
runners.comment: Last month's seasonal debut was not devoid of promise and she remains unexposed

runners.name: Mythical Bird
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; made the frame three consecutive times on AW in September/October (1m-1m2f) and is quite well handicapped on those efforts but not seen since a lesser run at the end of October.
runners.comment: Off since below-par run in October; has claims if judged on earlier form

runners.name: Lady McC
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in Ireland last year and absent since heavy defeat in AW claimer in October; fitted with tongue-tie for this stable debut; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: 0-5 in Ireland last year and absent since heavy defeat in AW claimer in October

runners.name: Ghost Story
runners.spotlight: In good form on soft turf during light campaign last year, most notably when winning over today's trip at Windsor; should be okay back on Polytrack but has fitness to prove after six-month break; market helpful.
runners.comment: In good form on soft turf during light campaign last year; back from six-month break

runners.name: Willow Wood
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly; didn't look anything special in her three 2yo races at around 1m but has plenty of scope for improvement in middle-distance handicaps this year; interesting.
runners.comment: Well-bred filly; showed only minor promise as a 2yo but ought to improve this year

races.race_name: Newsteam Group Handicap

runners.name: My Boy Harry
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f winner at Chelmsford in January, latterly off today's mark; also suited by 1m and is back in calmer waters after contesting a series final at Southwell this month (minus usual blinkers); interesting.
runners.comment: Dual 7f winner at Chelmsford in January, latterly off today's mark; 1m also suits

runners.name: Poke The Bear
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner (7f-1m) during the winter; 1lb lower than when second over C&D in February but needs to up his game after a couple of quiet runs.
runners.comment: Triple course winner during the winter; needs to raise game after two quiet runs

runners.name: Mr Fustic
runners.spotlight: Aided by new visor on his two starts this year, latterly when exploiting a much reduced mark at Southwell (1m) last month; that form has been well advertised by the runner-up since and this C&D also suits; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Aided by new visor this year and probably still on workable mark after last month's win

runners.name: Antiquity
runners.spotlight: Scored off 2lb lower over C&D in February and has remained in good form, most recently when fourth of 15 on turf (8.3f, good) three weeks ago; ought to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D in February and has remained in good form; in the mix again
runners.owner: P&h Moorhead, J Collins And S Piper

runners.name: Secret Road
runners.spotlight: Looked in excellent nick on last month's stable debut, gaining clearcut C&D win in first-time cheekpieces; not quite in same form when fourth off new mark here three weeks later but still considered.
runners.comment: Clear C&D winner on last month's stable debut; held since but still considered

runners.name: Super Hit
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Darryll Holland with a below-par run at Kempton in January but had gone close off today's mark over C&D a few days earlier and it will be interesting to see what James Owen can do with him (same owner).
runners.comment: Went close over C&D for Darryll Holland in January; interesting on debut for new stable

runners.name: Play Me
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with string of good efforts on turf, including a win at Epsom (8.5f good) and second in higher-grade company at Newmarket (1m, good); not yet proven on AW, though, and his fitness must be taken on trust.
runners.comment: Ended last season in good form on turf; back from a layoff; unproven on AW
runners.owner: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner

runners.name: Liberty Bird
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on some of last year's form for Charlie Johnston but finished down the field on last three outings, once for Alice Haynes and twice for Lemos De Souza; now with another new stable; too risky.
runners.comment: Seems badly to have lost his way; now with another new stable; too risky

runners.name: Dalmally
runners.spotlight: Bettered inauspicious seasonal/stable debut when a fairly respectable fifth of seven at Chelmsford (1m) last month but needs to do better again here; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Second stable run was much better than the first; needs more again today, though

runners.name: Far And Above
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who finished well down the field in first two handicaps (1m/1m3f); drops back in trip here; remains unexposed but could be considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Five-race maiden; well down the field in first two handicaps (1m/1m3f)

runners.name: Turpin
runners.spotlight: Belied 150-1 odds when fourth in 7f Kempton novice last spring but struggled in all three subsequent handicaps (7f-8.5f; gelded afterwards); returns from nine-month absence with huge amount to prove.
runners.comment: Struggled in first three handicaps; returns from nine-month absence

runners.name: Buy The Dip
runners.spotlight: Won off 1lb higher on turf here (7f) last summer and ended last season with respectable fourth at Brighton (1m) but this reappearance run is his first AW start since 2024 and his record when fresh is not compelling.
runners.comment: This seasonal debut is his first AW run since 2024; record when fresh is not compelling

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap

runners.name: Bella Bisbee
runners.spotlight: Built on earlier promise when scoring over C&D 16 days ago by 1l from Bold Suitor; up 4lb but she's not taken lightly in her current mood.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D 16 days ago; up 4lb but she's not taken lightly in her current mood

runners.name: Daisy Roots
runners.spotlight: Scored over 1m here in September and returned from six months off with a good fourth of 11 over C&D 16 days ago; well in the mix.
runners.comment: Returned from six months off with good C&D fourth 16 days ago; well in the mix

runners.name: Virtue Patience
runners.spotlight: Right back on track since undergoing wind surgery, a close third of eight in 1m handicap here in January; cheekpieces go on after a break and this C&D scorer can go well again.
runners.comment: Close third here in January; cheekpieces on after a break and he can go well again

runners.name: Portman Blue
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who wasn't discredited when fourth of nine in 1m Southwell novice last month; he may do better still now venturing into handicaps for the first time.
runners.comment: Not discredited when fourth at Southwell last month; may do better still on handicap debut

runners.name: Bold Suitor
runners.spotlight: A three-time 7f/1m AW winner in 2025; back on song when runner-up to Bella Bisbee over C&D 16 days ago, so must enter calculations nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Back on song when runner-up over C&D 16 days ago, so must enter calculations nudged up 1lb

runners.name: Blue Collar Lad
runners.spotlight: Course winner who returned from five months off with a very good second of nine over C&D last month, faring best of those held up; a player nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Course winner; off five months before a very good C&D second last month; a player

runners.name: Spirit Lead Me
runners.spotlight: A dual AW 7f/1m scorer in September but he's failed to beat a rival in his four starts this season, latest over C&D; tried in different headgear here.
runners.comment: A dual AW 7f/1m scorer in September but failed to beat a rival in four starts this season

runners.name: Havana Touch
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways in 1m Kempton handicap 29 days ago; up 4lb but must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Back to winning ways at Kempton 29 days ago; up 4lb but he must enter calculations

runners.name: Chola Empire
runners.spotlight: In good form (without winning) until a below-par sixth in 7f Chelmsford handicap in February; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: In good form until below-par sixth in Chelmsford h'cap in February; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Kit Gabriel
runners.spotlight: Got back on track when third of eight in 7f Wolverhampton handicap last month; eased 1lb and very much one to consider.
runners.comment: Got back on track with Wolverhampton 3rd latest; eased 1lb and very much one to consider

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Handicap

runners.name: Divot
runners.spotlight: Still seeking his first win after 13 attempts but he has finished second on three occasions, including at Wolverhampton on his seasonal return three weeks ago (1m4f; hooded first time); that was a costly reverse for those that backed him into 11-10f but he didn't do a great deal wrong and the subsequent wins of the first and third give the form some ballast; major player with the hood now discarded.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden but latest second has been well franked and he's a key player once again

runners.name: Upepo
runners.spotlight: Well backed when winning over C&D in December off a 2lb lower mark (ridden by Myla Coppins); the odd solid effort since and he can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Ready C&D win in December off 2lb lower; the odd solid effort since; not ruled out

runners.name: Sir Patchy
runners.spotlight: Still seeking his first win but he was placed twice in Kempton handicaps last October (1m/1m4f) after a lengthy layoff; 148-day absence small fry in comparison and he could still have a bigger effort in him.
runners.comment: Lightly raced for his age; two Kempton runs (1m/1m4f) last year that give him good claims

runners.name: Fullforward
runners.spotlight: Just low-level hurdle form on his last two starts; last year's Flat best, which came over C&D, would give him a squeak but others look stronger for win purposes.
runners.comment: Effective over C&D and on a lowly mark; not dismissed but others appeal more for the win
