runners.name: Imperial Cult
runners.spotlight: Won three times in the summer of 2024, including twice over C&D; several good runs in defeat last year and he starts out for George Baker (same owner) off a competitive mark; one to consider.
runners.comment: Two of his three wins have come over C&D; starts out for new yard off a dangerous mark

runners.name: Skimming Along
runners.spotlight: Improved for a step up in trip/switch to handicaps last summer, winning twice on turf at around this trip (good to firm and heavy) before ending 2025 with a close second at Chelmsford (1m6f); still has potential after just nine starts and he's an interesting contender.
runners.comment: Did well when upped in trip/handicapping last year, winning twice; still has low mileage

runners.name: Lunar Power
runners.spotlight: Losing run stretches back to May 2023 (hurdles); ended 2025 with some modest efforts for James Owen and fared little better when fifth of seven on his stable debut 11 weeks ago (2m, Polytrack); enough to prove.
runners.comment: Regressive 8yo; needs to leave February's stable debut well behind him

runners.name: The Colorist
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in September 2024; the odd good run in early 2025 but this habitual slow-starter always comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: C&D winner; often blows the start and therefore always carries risk

races.race_name: Racing TV Club Day Here Today Handicap

runners.name: Karakula Dancer
runners.spotlight: Big improver on AW this year and made it 3-6 for 2026 when winning at Wolverhampton (8.6f) this month; leading player if able to transfer his form back to turf.
runners.comment: Three AW wins this year and he's a leading player if transferring his form back to turf

runners.name: Kameko Fever
runners.spotlight: Went close at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March on second handicap start and he's of interest on that form, but he was then a soundly beaten fourth of ten at Musselburgh (1m, good) this month on his first run on turf; now 0-6.
runners.comment: Went close on AW in March but soundly beaten on subsequent turf debut at Musselburgh
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Walk On Walk On
runners.spotlight: Displayed some ability last year but went 0-6 and he was well beaten at 28-1 on his recent stable and seasonal debut at Ripon.
runners.comment: Made it 0-7 when well beaten at 28-1 on his recent stable and seasonal debut at Ripon

runners.name: Fareenar
runners.spotlight: Steady improvement across her three 2yo starts, all here, finishing third in a C&D novice (good) last October when last seen; begins handicap life on a fair mark and in a first-time tongue-tie; not discounted.
runners.comment: Third in C&D novice last October when last seen and begins handicap life on a fair mark

runners.name: Rocklaw
runners.spotlight: Made debut in February and took a step forward when fourth of six at Thirsk (1m, good) three weeks ago on her third run, when the steady pace appeared to be against her; goes handicapping today and she's open to improvement.
runners.comment: Steady pace appeared against her at Thirsk; goes handicapping and is open to improvement

runners.name: Lope Y Linda
runners.spotlight: Ran six times last year and finished no better than fifth, including in three nurseries in the autumn; improvement needed on reappearance.
runners.comment: Ran six times last year and finished no better than fifth; improvement needed

runners.name: Ice Cube
runners.spotlight: Improved upon first two runs when seventh of eight in warm maiden at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last September in a first-time hood (left off today); gelded since; pedigree provides hope that he will be better than his opening mark and connections had a 3yo handicap newcomer win over C&D last week.
runners.comment: Useful pedigree and connections had a 3yo handicap newcomer win easily over C&D last week

runners.name: Mohaasset
runners.spotlight: Fairly encouraging debut run at Newbury (5f, good) last April; two lesser runs followed (6f/7f, good/soft) and she was sold out of Darryll Holland's yard for 1,000gns last September; however, she's bred to be suited by the step up in trip on handicap/stable debut and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Failed to build on initial 2yo promise; bred to be suited by new trip on h'cap/yard debut

runners.name: Ephron
runners.spotlight: Down the field on his three 2yo starts (7f/6f) and improvement is needed off his opening mark; however, he cost 52,000euros at the breeze-ups last May and could be capable of better; gelded since last time; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Down the field on his three 2yo starts; could be capable of better; watch the betting
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxvi And P Demilow

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hungarian
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in defeat on all four starts last year, finishing fourth of 26 in a sales race at Newmarket (7f, good) in October when last seen; first-time cheekpieces replace the blinkers adopted last time; leading claims if at the top of his game on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Promise in defeat on all four starts last year; leading form claims on this reappearance

runners.name: Pacific Glory
runners.spotlight: 3,500gns foal; eighth foal; half-brother to five winners including Nairai (French 9.7f/1m4f), Fedya (French 1m3f) and Star's Of Pride (German 7.5f/1m); dam US 6f 2yo winner; trainer won this last year but this Masar gelding may need further than today's debut trip.
runners.comment: Trainer won this last year but this Masar gelding may need further than today's debut trip

runners.name: Elias Escape
runners.spotlight: £48,000 yearling, £40,000 2yo breeze-ups; ninth foal; half-brother to several winners, the best of whom on the Flat was Happaugue (10.6f AW; RPR 74); dam 1m winner (67); market check advised on debut but useful standard needed.
runners.comment: Half-brother to several winners; market check advised on debut but useful standard needed

runners.name: Whernside
runners.spotlight: Left his sole 2yo run (1m, AW) well behind when runner-up at Newcastle (7f, AW) this month; open to further improvement for trainer who won this in 2024 and he's one to consider on his turf debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up on AW this month on second start and he's open to further improvement
runners.owner: Unique Retreats Stays Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Lucky Hero
runners.spotlight: Made it 0-7 when below par in Goodwood nursery last October when last seen; gelded since; however, he finished in the first three on five occasions last season and his trainer won this in 2022 and 2023; possible player.
runners.comment: 0-7 last year but in the first three on five occasions; has been gelded; possible player

races.race_name: Start Racing TV Free Trial Now Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Egotistical
runners.spotlight: Placed on first two starts in the winter and improved form when close third on recent turf debut at Nottingham (6f, good); she wasn't stopping at the line last time and there's every chance she'll stay 7f; key player.
runners.comment: Close third over 6f at Nottingham and there's every chance she'll stay 7f; key player

runners.name: Maldevious
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who was down the field at Musselburgh (7f, good) 18 days ago but that may have come too soon, just two days after her close fourth of 15 at Thirsk (6f, good); stays 7f and might not be far away.
runners.comment: 0-8 but had an excuse last time, after good run previously, and she might not be far away

runners.name: Why Because
runners.spotlight: 0-4 for Harry Charlton; changed hands twice since, for 6,000gns on December 1 and 1,000gns on December 11; her best form reads well in today's context and she's not ruled out on her stable debut.
runners.comment: 0-4 for Harry Charlton but best form reads well in today's context; makes stable debut

runners.name: Confide In Me
runners.spotlight: Unraced dam from a good Cheveley Park family but didn't make the track for them and bought for 2,500gns last December; 9-1 when winning on this month's debut at Southwell (7f, AW), doing well to come from further behind than the other principals in a slowly run race; respected under 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Did well to win on this month's debut at Southwell and she's respected under a 7lb penalty

runners.name: Viviana
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 6f/7f maiden (RPR 60), sister to 5f/6f Group 3 winner Judicial, half-sister to Nunthorpe winner Marsha, out of 5f 2yo Listed winner from family of top miler Soviet Song; no surprise to see a big run on debut.
runners.comment: Dam from classy Elite Racing family; no surprise to see a big run on debut

runners.name: Prima Domina
runners.spotlight: Kept on for second on debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) last October, beaten about 5l at 12-1, then reportedly lost her action when well beaten there (6f) at 25-1; she's unexposed and it's possible 7f will suit, but others have more substance to their claims.
runners.comment: Displayed promise on the first of her two runs but others have more substance to claims

runners.name: Highfield Jewel
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability at Southwell this winter on her first two starts (7f/6f, AW) but not enough to suggest she's the one to be on today.
runners.comment: Showed some ability at Southwell this winter but not enough to suggest she's the one to be

races.race_name: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap

runners.name: Beerwah
runners.spotlight: Just one win from his 17 starts but that came last September at Beverley and he was beaten just a neck on his reappearance at Doncaster (5f, good to firm) last Friday; there's every chance he'll be bang there once more.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate but beaten just a neck on last Friday's reappearance at Doncaster

runners.name: Doralee
runners.spotlight: Her 18 races have yielded just one win but that came over C&D (good to firm) last August; close third of 14 at Doncaster last Friday (5f, good to firm; one place behind Beerwah) and she may again be firmly involved.
runners.comment: Has won just once but that was over C&D; close third last Friday; could be firmly involved

runners.name: Solar Biricz
runners.spotlight: Patchy form across final few runs for Brian Ellison last season and well beaten on recent stable/seasonal debut at Thirsk (6f, good); however, he's 5lb lower than when winning at Doncaster (5f, good to form) last June and is not a forlorn hope back at 5f in a first-time visor.
runners.comment: Well beaten on stable/seasonal debut but well treated and not a forlorn hope back at 5f

runners.name: Miss Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Four wins last June/July (5f-7f) and this front-runner returned to form when third at Catterick (6f, good) three weeks ago on her second start of the season; she can make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Four wins last summer and she returned to form at Catterick three weeks ago; solid claims

runners.name: Soul Seeker
runners.spotlight: He's on a basement mark but this 9yo was sharply regressive last season and well beaten on his reappearance at Catterick three weeks ago.
runners.comment: On a basement mark but sharply regressive last year and well beaten on reappearance

runners.name: Kyber Crystal
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent in 2025 and she finished last on her final two outings in November; however, one of her two wins last year came on her reappearance in April, so she's capable when fresh; not discounted.
runners.comment: Inconsistent last year but won on reappearance last April so she's capable when fresh

runners.name: Zuffolo
runners.spotlight: Last season ended with two disappointing runs and there was no return to form on his recent reappearance; however, he may have needed that latest outing and this two-time C&D winner is back down to last September's last winning mark; not written off.
runners.comment: No return to form on recent reappearance but this two-time C&D winner is not written off
runners.owner: R R D Saunders And Partner

runners.name: Sanbona
runners.spotlight: 5f wins on AW/turf in August/September last year and he's on a handy mark on that form; however, his campaign concluded with two disappointing runs and he needs to get back on track on this reappearance outing.
runners.comment: Won last August/September but campaign concluded with two disappointing runs

runners.name: Mrs Trump
runners.spotlight: Two-time course winner, including this race in 2024, and she posted wins at Thirsk and Nottingham last July; lacks a recent run but can be in the mix if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2024; lacks a recent run but can be in the mix if fully tuned up
runners.owner: R Chapman, G J Daly & R J Parks

runners.name: Desert Champion
runners.spotlight: On a reduced mark and he showed some spark last November; however, he was disappointing in the main in the second half of last year and finished down the field on his last two starts; returns with something to prove.
runners.comment: On a reduced mark but disappointing in the main in the second half of last year

races.race_name: Racing TV Free Trial Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Austrian Theory
runners.spotlight: 0-10 last year but placed on final two runs of the season last autumn and he didn't run badly at Ripon a fortnight ago on his reappearance; his last win came off this mark and he could improve for that recent outing.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly at Ripon on reappearance and could improve for it; last winning mark

runners.name: Fierce Fortitude
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fourth attempt in a maiden at Brighton (7f, good to firm) last September and he reappeared with a close second on last month's handicap debut at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW); that latest form reads well and this unexposed 4yo is a major player up 2lb.
runners.comment: Close second on last month's handicap debut (AW) and that form reads well; major player

runners.name: Raulin
runners.spotlight: Went up the weights having won three times last year, including C&D (good to firm) in May; unplaced on final start last term and same story on recent reappearance, but he may have needed that latest run and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Three wins last year, including C&D; could improve for recent comeback run; not ruled out

runners.name: Project Geofin
runners.spotlight: Tailed off last November when last seen but enjoyed a good season in the main otherwise, winning two in a row in the summer and finishing second over C&D (good) on his penultimate start; his record when fresh in the spring is pretty decent and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Good season in the main last year and his record when fresh in the spring is pretty decent

runners.name: Ammes
runners.spotlight: Caught wide when down the field at Lingfield in February on last Flat start and when well beaten over hurdles on his last two runs he was contesting hot 4yo races; on a competitive mark on last summer's Flat form and he was a Listed hurdle runner-up in October; not ruled out back in this code.
runners.comment: Tough tasks over hurdles on last two starts; competitive mark on last summer's Flat form

runners.name: Urban Road
runners.spotlight: Beaten on his last 13 starts going back to last February and eight of his nine wins have come on AW; can be slowly away but that didn't stop him finishing second over C&D (good to firm) last Monday when back up in trip; has the same mark today and could well be in the mix once more.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D last Monday and could well be in the mix once more
runners.owner: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Thunder Wonder
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on last season's peak efforts and he took a step back in the right direction when fourth of 12 at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) recently; however, that track brings out the best in him, he needs to build on that latest run, and he's unproven at 1m2f.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction when 4th at Musselburgh; has to build on it; unproven at 1m2f

runners.name: James McHenry
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in the Cambridgeshire (1m1f) in September 2024 and he's raced just four times since; denied clear run when keeping on for fifth of 12 at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) recently and the return to 1m2f is worth a go; could have a part to play in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Lightly raced since 2024 Cambridgeshire 2nd; positive performance at Musselburgh recently

runners.name: Diderot
runners.spotlight: Not the force of old on AW and he refused to race at Lingfield in February; made it 0-7 on turf when 5l sixth of nine here (1m, good to firm) last Monday but that wasn't devoid of promise as he was denied a clear run and kept on pretty nicely; he's not ruled out each-way back up in trip.
runners.comment: 0-7 on turf but not devoid of promise here last Monday and he's not ruled out each-way

races.race_name: Watch Race Replays At racingtv.com Handicap

runners.name: King Of Berkshire
runners.spotlight: Camelot colt who improved upon his 7f/1m maiden form when short-headed on recent handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm), where he was nicely clear of the third; returns to action before a rise in the weights kicks in and he's a very strong candidate.
runners.comment: Short-headed at Yarmouth recently and he's ahead of the handicapper today

runners.name: Just A Gambler
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten when back on turf at Musselburgh (1m, good) this month but may have been unsuited by first-time cheekpieces (which are now left off); running well over 1m on AW previously, when shaping as though this longer trip may suit, and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: In good form on AW then soundly beaten back on turf, but cheekpieces may not have suited

runners.name: Yorkies Dream
runners.spotlight: She's raced three times (7f-8.4f) and her most promising effort came when keeping on for third of five over 7f here last October on debut; this Ulysses filly could improve for the step up in trip on handicap debut and is one to watch in the betting.
runners.comment: Ulysses filly who could improve for the step up in trip on h'cap debut; check the betting
runners.owner: T Cottier, On Top Racing, Amity Fin

runners.name: Sudbury Hill
runners.spotlight: Went close on stable debut at Kempton (1m, AW) in February and again ran well when third of six at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) latest; however, it's possible he's better at a shorter trip and best form thus far has come on AW.
runners.comment: In good form on AW but return to turf and the 1m2f trip are both question marks

runners.name: Fairydale
runners.spotlight: Finished held in fifth last September in her two nurseries (8.4f/1m); however, she showed signs of promise previously and, being by Waldgeist, the step up in trip may well prove to be a positive move on her reappearance, for which she wears first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Only fifth in her two nurseries but by Waldgeist and could appreciate this longer trip

runners.name: Red Rifle
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten sixth of eight on nursery debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) last October but that doesn't tell the full story as he came home first of the three that raced on the near side; reappears with trainer in fine form and it will be no surprise to see him give a good account.
runners.comment: His nursery debut defeat doesn't tell the full story; reappears with trainer in fine form

races.race_name: Set Your Limits With Unibet Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Invincible Isaac
runners.spotlight: Mayson colt; rallying fourth of five, beaten only about 2l, at Nottingham (5f) to step up on his Brocklesby effort; may improve again, with this extra furlong a likely plus.
runners.comment: Rallying fourth of five at Nottingham to step up on his Brocklesby effort, both over 5f

runners.name: Anna Belardo
runners.spotlight: Belardo colt; showed ability with third of five, albeit about 4l behind the winner, in 5f event at the Newmarket Craven meeting; ran to an ordinary RPR but may record a bigger figure second time out.
runners.comment: Showed ability with third of five in 5f event at Newmarket; may show improvement

runners.name: Duidin
runners.spotlight: February foal; 18,000euros foal, £22,000 yearling; by Cotai Glory out of unraced half-sister to three winners, notably Cecchini (1m 2yo); booking of Oisin Murphy (good strike-rate for yard) bodes well; market confidence would heighten interest.
runners.comment: £22,000 yearling; booking of Oisin Murphy (good strike-rate for yard) bodes well

runners.name: Forest Berry
runners.spotlight: January foal; by Waldgeist; sibling to two winners, notably Native American (French 1m2f including Listed; RPR 108); dam US 6.5f turf scorer (including 2yo/stakes); heed the market signals.
runners.comment: Waldgeist filly; siblings include a 1m2f Listed winner; heed the market signals

runners.name: Last Dandelion
runners.spotlight: March foal; 4,000gns yearling; by Pinatubo; sibling to winners Gatecrasher Girl (1m-8.6f including AW; RPR 87) and Beach Partee (5f/6f 2yo including AW; 63); dam 6f 2yo scorer (83); yard's first 2yo runner of 2026; market instructive.
runners.comment: 4,000gns yearling; by Pinatubo; yard's first 2yo runner of 2026; market instructive

runners.name: Angel Sense
runners.spotlight: April foal; £38,000 yearling; by Harry Angel and first foal of 6f-7.4f winner (including AW 2yo; RPR 83); very suitably bred and has a ground-saving draw; interesting, especially if the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: £38,000 yearling; by Harry Angel out of 6f-7.4f winner (including AW 2yo); interesting

runners.name: Kalahari Flame
runners.spotlight: March foal; 11,000gns foal, £21,000 yearling; Sergei Prokofiev half-sister to 5.7f 2yo winner Reality Queen (RPR 60); dam prolific 5f/6f scorer (including 2yo/AW; 84); likely sort judged on pedigree.
runners.comment: £21,000 yearling; by Sergei Prokofiev and related to 2yo winners; looks a likely sort

runners.name: Graceful George
runners.spotlight: January foal; 9,000gns yearling; Inns Of Court half-brother to two winners, notably Our Mighty Mo (6f/7f 2yo; RPR 83); dam 6f/7f 2yo maiden (67); a likelier type than stablemate Ongombo on breeding.
runners.comment: 9,000gns yearling; a likelier type than stablemate Ongombo on breeding

runners.name: Location Location
runners.spotlight: March foal; 10,000gns foal, 35,000gns yearling; by Harry Angel; dam 5f 2yo scorer (RPR 73), half-sister to to 5.5f/6f 2yo winner She's A Gambler; yard's first 2yo runner of the campaign; market helpful.
runners.comment: 35,000gns yearling; yard's first 2yo runner of the campaign; market helpful

runners.name: Ongombo
runners.spotlight: March foal; 50,000gns yearling; by Siyouni; dam 1m2f/1m4f AW scorer (RPR 78), half-sister to several winners, notably Highland Chief (1m4f US Grade 1); may be one for longer trips later on.
runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; pedigree suggests he may be one for longer trips later on

runners.name: O'Gorman
runners.spotlight: Sergei Prokofiev colt; finished 4l behind the winner but pulled 3l clear of the third in 6f contest at Yarmouth, showing some promise while recording a modest RPR; may improve with the experience under his belt.
runners.comment: Finished 4l behind the winner but pulled 3l clear of the third in 6f contest at Yarmouth

races.race_name: Unibet Supports Safer Gambling Handicap

runners.name: Blue Celestial
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on earlier promise when beating one on her handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m) 19 days ago; she needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Failed to build on earlier promise when beating one on h'cap debut at Yarmouth 19 days ago

runners.name: Slack Bob
runners.spotlight: Advanced his form when a strong-travelling runner-up in 1m Yarmouth handicap 19 days ago, edged out only close home; a likely player nudged up 1lb in his bid to get off the mark at the seventh attempt.
runners.comment: Very good Yarmouth runner-up latest; likely player up 1lb in his bid to get off the mark
runners.owner: The Old Etonian Racing Syndicate II

runners.name: Wild Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Vastly improved after ten months off (gelded) when a wide-margin winner on his handicap debut at Redcar (1m) and he easily followed up under a 6lb penalty at Lingfield (1m) two days ago; saddles a double penalty now but still very hard to oppose.
runners.comment: Wide-margin Redcar winner and easily followed up at Lingfield two days ago; hard to oppose

runners.name: Station Bar
runners.spotlight: Resumed with a good fourth over 1m here but he went too keenly when only sixth in 1m Yarmouth handicap 19 days ago; no surprise to see him bounce back now dropped back in trip.
runners.comment: Too keen when sixth over 1m at Yarmouth 19 days ago; can bounce back now back at 7f

runners.name: Sup Of Red
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who has been in good form since joining her current handler, runner-up in 7f Wolverhampton handicap three weeks ago; she ought to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: In good form for current yard, runner-up at Wolverhampton latest; ought to be thereabouts

runners.name: Roccobear
runners.spotlight: Calyx gelding who has made a solid start since joining his current yard, fifth of ten in 7f Lingfield handicap 16 days ago; no forlorn hope eased 1lb here.
runners.comment: Solid fifth of ten in 7f Lingfield handicap 16 days ago; no forlorn hope eased 1lb here

runners.name: Kingofthecarnival
runners.spotlight: Emphatically got off the mark at Newcastle (7f) on his return but he went too freely when only seventh (6-5 favourite) in 1m handicap there later in March; he's worth another chance dropped back in trip.
runners.comment: Ready Newcastle winner but too free over 1m there since; worth another chance back to 7f

runners.name: Superstorm
runners.spotlight: Gelded/off four months before posting a good third of seven in C&D handicap on his return last month; took a strong hold there so could build on it with cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Gelded/off four months before good C&D third on return; cheekpieces on; could do better

runners.name: Hello Humphrey
runners.spotlight: He's 0-7 but proved a reliable juvenile and signed off for 2025 with a good third of eight in C&D nursery in December; not out of things after a break.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with good C&D nursery third in December; not out of things after a break

runners.name: Fullstop
runners.spotlight: Bungleinthejungle gelding who took a step forward (first-time hood) under more positive tactics when third of 17 in 7f Leicester handicap 17 days ago; not out of things off the same mark with the headgear retained.
runners.comment: Posted a good Leicester third latest; in the mix off same mark with headgear retained

runners.name: Personal Pride
runners.spotlight: A'Ali gelding; has been brought along steadily, nearest at the finish when third of eight in 7f Wolverhampton maiden in December; back from a break with more to offer on his first go in handicap company with Jamie Spencer now up.
runners.comment: Brought along steadily, third at 7f Wolverhampton last time; more to offer on h'cap debut

runners.name: That's Enough
runners.spotlight: Hinted at promise on her first two runs but cheekpieces on when last of 13 in C&D maiden in January; she needs this switch to handicaps to spark major progress.
runners.comment: Last of 13 in C&D maiden in January; needs switch to handicaps to spark major progress
runners.owner: Commercium II And Whitsbury Manor Stud

runners.name: Ethereal Charm
runners.spotlight: Only modest form shown in a trio of 1m novices at two, too free when seventh at Bath in October; she needs to take a big step forward now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Only modest form in trio of novices at two; needs to take big step forward on h'cap debut

runners.name: Summer Oasis
runners.spotlight: Failed to offer much in her three outings last autumn, beating one in C&D novice in October; handicap debutante.
runners.comment: Failed to offer much in three outings last autumn, beating one in C&D novice in October

races.race_name: Unibet 3 Uniboosts A Day Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tales Of Wisdom
runners.spotlight: Godolphin colt who, despite taking a good hold in the early stages, easily justified favouritism at Southwell (7f) where there was 11l back to the runner-up; may settle better with hood now fitted; promising.
runners.comment: Took a good hold at Southwell but won by 11l; hood is enlisted; promising sort

runners.name: Trelissick
runners.spotlight: Juddmonte filly by Kingman; sister to 7f AW winner Upscale (RPR 90); dam 7f scorer (including AW 2yo/Group 3; 107); wears tongue-tie on debut; very suitably bred and looks interesting.
runners.comment: Juddmonte filly who is very suitably bred and looks an interesting debutante

runners.name: Langstone
runners.spotlight: Gelded after sole 2yo start; looked less than straightforward at Doncaster (upped to 7f) on reappearance but managed to get his neck in front close home and may build on that success; one of the main players.
runners.comment: Looked less than straightforward at Doncaster on reappearance but may build on that win

runners.name: Ray Mon Dough
runners.spotlight: Beat only three rivals at Lingfield (6f, AW) in sole 2yo outing but had them well strung out and registered an emphatic success from the front, looking a useful prospect; likely to stay 7f; commands respect.
runners.comment: Had his three rivals well strung out at Lingfield, looking a useful prospect; respected

runners.name: Brave New World
runners.spotlight: 220,000gns yearling; by Siyouni; sibling to six winners, notably Walk In Marrakesh (7f/7.4f 2yo including Listed; RPR 99); dam maiden (82) half-sister to 5.5f Group 2 scorer Zipping; check the betting.
runners.comment: 220,000gns yearling; by Siyouni; sibling to six winners; check the betting

runners.name: Cosmic
runners.spotlight: May be sharper and wiser with Lingfield effort (6f, AW) under her belt but needs a marked step forward, having finished last of five; perhaps one for handicaps later on.
runners.comment: Last of five at Lingfield; needs a marked step forward

runners.name: I'm Not Leaving
runners.spotlight: 48,000euros yearling; by Oasis Dream out of 7f AW winner (RPR 98); suitably bred but will likely need to be useful to make a winning debut in this field; market should offer further guidance.
runners.comment: 48,000euros yearling; suitably bred but will need to be useful to win this race on debut

runners.name: Mr Jeffrey
runners.spotlight: Ulysses gelding; dam 7f/1m scorer (including AW; RPR 91); yard not associated with winning newcomers.
runners.comment: Yard not associated with winning newcomers

runners.name: Special Gold
runners.spotlight: Havana Gold gelding; dam 6f 2yo scorer (RPR 84), half-sister to two useful winners; probably best watched in this field on debut.
runners.comment: Havana Gold gelding; probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Flying Squad
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to a Kempton reappearance effort (1m) that suggested he'll be suited by this return to 7f; could go well, albeit gives the impression he'll be more interesting in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Kempton reappearance effort suggested this drop back to 7f will suit

runners.name: Golden Conqueror
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns breeze-up purchase whose September promise comprised an eye-catching effort over C&D and comfortable success in small field at Newcastle (7f); this is harder but he may improve further.
runners.comment: Eye-catcher over C&D then scored comfortably at Newcastle; may improve further

runners.name: Makerstoun
runners.spotlight: 500,000euros yearling; by Night Of Thunder; shaped better than the bare result (sixth of eight) in 7f maiden at the Newmarket Craven meeting; open to improvement with the experience under his belt.
runners.comment: Shaped better than bare result in maiden at the Craven meeting; open to improvement

runners.name: Anton Mauve
runners.spotlight: Twice went through the sales for six figures; only eighth of nine in 7f contest at Wolverhampton but this Amo runner may do better with the experience under his belt.
runners.comment: Only eight of nine at Wolverhampton but may do better

runners.name: Tiger's Nest
runners.spotlight: Shaped encouragingly with keeping-on third of nine over C&D in sole 2yo start; recorded an ordinary RPR but may well post bigger figures this term; certainly needs such progress to beat the previous winners.
runners.comment: Shaped encouragingly, albeit recorded ordinary RPR, over C&D in sole 2yo start

races.race_name: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Merry
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in 7f contests at Kempton and Southwell; not crying out for this extra furlong judged on pedigree but she is the form pick; admittedly sets only an ordinary standard.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in 7f contests at Kempton and Southwell; the form pick
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing And R Mitchell

runners.name: Premier Cru
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise with fifth of 13 over C&D, then raced freely and looked a non-stayer over 1m2f at Yarmouth; may show progress returned to this scenario with hood applied.
runners.comment: May show progress returned to this C&D with hood applied

runners.name: Royal Fanfare
runners.spotlight: Kingman filly; dam 7f-1m2f scorer (including Listed; RPR 102), sister to 1m2f AW winners Suitor and Plead, half-sister to winners Golden Horde (Commonwealth Cup), Camille Pissarro (7f 2yo Group 1) and Line Of Departure (6f Listed); notable connections; interesting debutante.
runners.comment: Debutante by Kingman out of well-related Listed winner; notable connections; interesting

runners.name: Lightning Glory
runners.spotlight: 5,000euros foal, 1,200euros yearling, 165,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; by Cotai Glory; dam unplaced 7f/1m AW 2yo (RPR 60), half-sister to useful US 1m/1m1f winner K P All Systems Go; one of several newcomers in the field; market informative.
runners.comment: 165,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; one of several newcomers in the field; market informative
runners.owner: Duaij Al Khalifa & Ahmad Al Qassimi

runners.name: Star Baby
runners.spotlight: 32,000gns foal, 75,000euros yearling; by Zoustar out of French 9.5f/1m2f AW winner (including Listed; RPR 94); will act well on the surface if taking after her dam; the betting may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: 75,000euros yearling; by Zoustar out of AW winner; check the betting

runners.name: Zarique
runners.spotlight: Newcomer by Zarak and first foal of 7f/1m-winning (including US; RPR 96) daughter of French Listed-placed 1m4f scorer; in good hands; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: Newcomer by Zarak and first foal of 7f/1m scorer; heed the market signals

runners.name: Sharp Romance
runners.spotlight: Shaped encouragingly with fourth of 11, best of the newcomers, in 1m event at Yarmouth; recorded a modest RPR but may improve with the experience under her belt; out of an AW scorer.
runners.comment: Shaped encouragingly with fourth of 11, best of the newcomers in 1m event at Yarmouth

runners.name: Midnight Steppers
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form, having achieved zilch in 1m event at Nottingham.
runners.comment: Achieved zilch in 1m event at Nottingham

runners.name: Rajwaah
runners.spotlight: Only sixth of seven in 7f maiden at Kempton but seemed to need the outing/experience; from a good family and looks open to improvement.
runners.comment: Seemed to need the outing/experience in 7f maiden at Kempton

runners.name: Mmmbop
runners.spotlight: Finished fifth of nine in C&D maiden, faring best of the runners who started at triple-digit odds but recording a meagre RPR; perhaps one for handicaps later on.
runners.comment: Finished fifth of nine in C&D maiden, recording a meagre RPR

runners.name: Lissahane Lass
runners.spotlight: Holds very weak claims after two bumpers and Tuesday's 1m AW maiden.
runners.comment: Holds very weak claims after two bumpers and Tuesday's 1m AW maiden

runners.name: Hotel California
runners.spotlight: Only eighth of 14 in 1m event at Southwell in sole 2yo start but is evidently thought capable of better, having attracted support.
runners.comment: Comfortably held at Southwell but attracted support and may do better
runners.owner: C Wright, D Kilburn & N Hutchings

races.race_name: Get Best Odds Guaranteed At Unibet Handicap

runners.name: Sea Founder
runners.spotlight: Versatile and in-form 4yo; opened his AW account in Lingfield contest (1m2f) in February; sound efforts at Kempton (1m) and Lingfield (1m4f; Good Friday card) since, finishing fourth both times; solid claims back down in distance.
runners.comment: Versatile and in-form 4yo who holds solid claims back down in distance

runners.name: I Am Me
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 5331, mostly over C&D, for current stable; got up on the line when last seen two months ago; may improve further on AW and warrants respect.
runners.comment: Record of 5331, mostly over C&D, for current stable; may improve further on AW; respected

runners.name: Jazzy Baby
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-7, the success in a winter maiden, but she has proved consistent, mostly over C&D; likely to give running and again has frame possibilities.
runners.comment: Record is only 1-7 but she has proved consistent, mostly over C&D; frame possibilities

runners.name: Emery Down
runners.spotlight: Kempton maiden winner (7f) for previous yard; 0-9 in handicaps but seemed a non-stayer over 1m2f at Doncaster (debut for new stable) most recently; mark is workable on best form.
runners.comment: 0-9 in handicaps but is well treated on best form; second start for new stable

runners.name: Berry Clever
runners.spotlight: Has gained all three wins at Kempton, the most recent on final start in 2025; respectable third kept to C&D on reappearance; in the mix, albeit is still seeking a first success outside of the month of October.
runners.comment: All wins at Kempton; respectable third kept to C&D on reappearance; in the mix

runners.name: Tactical Plan
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on turf and over 7f, winning twice (as a 2yo); latest effort was better than reappearance and suggested this return to 1m is worth exploring; could go well, provided he's as effective back on AW.
runners.comment: Latest 7f effort suggested this return to 1m is worth exploring; could go well

runners.name: Ash Wednesday
runners.spotlight: Has done most of his racing over 7f, winning six times (all on AW); back in form with placed efforts on last two starts, latest at Kempton; close third at this venue in sole 1m attempt; again has an each-way shout.
runners.comment: Six-time winner over 7f; close third at Kempton in sole 1m attempt; each-way shout

runners.name: Celestias Comet
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly whose Southwell novice win last September took her 1m record to 221; finished last in middle-distance events (Listed/handicap) since but may prove resurgent back down in trip, provided the removal of usual hood doesn't backfire.
runners.comment: May prove resurgent back at 1m; record over this trip reads 221; usual hood removed
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Dowling And & Mrs Marney

runners.name: Billy Mill
runners.spotlight: Has done most of his winning over 7f, most recently off current mark at Kempton in January; however, posted best effort since when keeping-on third over C&D last time; fighting chance.
runners.comment: Won off current mark at Kempton in January; placed over C&D last time; fighting chance

runners.name: Atlantis Blue
runners.spotlight: Absent since an autumn C&D effort that was respectable but took her AW record to 0-9; has gained none of her turf wins when fresh; doesn't look the percentage call.
runners.comment: Absent since an autumn C&D effort that was respectable but took her AW record to 0-9

runners.name: Sixfiveseven
runners.spotlight: Has shown best form, notably Newmarket success for Keiran Burke, on turf; absent since 2024 and has enough to prove kept to AW on debut for new yard; still in same ownership.
runners.comment: Best form on turf; absent since 2024; debut for new yard; enough to prove

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Space Bear
runners.spotlight: Proved more settled than usual when readily getting off the mark for her new yard in 6f Yarmouth handicap nine days ago; the one to beat under a 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: More settled when a ready scorer at Yarmouth nine days ago; the one to beat under 6lb pen

runners.name: Leonie
runners.spotlight: Made debut last month and has shown some promise when in the frame on all three runs, not enjoying the smoothest of passages when third of five in 6f Lingfield maiden 16 days ago; this Ubettabelieveit filly has more to offer on her handicap debut; likely player.
runners.comment: Showed some promise when in the frame on her three runs; more to offer on handicap debut

runners.name: Tenzi
runners.spotlight: Pinatubo filly who built on earlier promise when landing the odds in 7f Southwell novice last month; not out of things now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Landed odds in 7f Southwell novice last month; not out of things now going handicapping

runners.name: Portacloy
runners.spotlight: Landed 6f Lingfield maiden in September but she failed to kick on sent handicapping, beating one at Wolverhampton (6f) the following month; has something to prove on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield but failed to kick on sent handicapping; something to prove on return

runners.name: Perfect Location
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f winner at two; not disgraced after seven months off when a fading sixth in C&D handicap 22 days ago; can take a step forward with a tongue tie now added.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when C&D sixth 22 days ago; can take step forward with a tongue tie added

runners.name: Bessie Wallis
runners.spotlight: Opened her account in 5f novice at Lingfield in October but she failed to beat a rival on her nursery debut at Wolverhampton (5f) in December; needs to hit the ground running after a break.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival on nursery debut at Wolverhampton in December; lots more needed

races.race_name: Try Unibet's Superboosts Handicap

runners.name: Blackisthenewblack
runners.spotlight: Ties in with two of tonight's rivals as she finished alongside Wedonttellthetruth in the C&D event won by Cospicua when making handicap/seasonal debut; may build on that effort and show progress, provided she takes well to first-time headgear; shortlisted.
runners.comment: May build on her effort in the C&D event won by Cospicua; one to consider
runners.owner: Team Valor, Cayton Park Stud, A Singh

runners.name: Cospicua
runners.spotlight: Came with a strong late surge to lead on the line last time, taking record over C&D to 2-2 and making it 2-3 in handicaps, for a sizeable LSP to boot (gained the wins at odds of 25-1 and 12-1); respected kept to this scenario, with further improvement plausible.
runners.comment: Got up on the line last time, taking C&D record to 2-2; may improve further; respected

runners.name: Wedonttellthetruth
runners.spotlight: Has worn cheekpieces since switched to handicaps and upped to 1m4f, winning at Wolverhampton then creditable sixth to Cospicua at Kempton; chance of reversing the placings partly depends on how well he takes to a headgear swap (now blinkered).
runners.comment: Chance of reversing placings with Cospicua depends on the effects of headgear switch

runners.name: Port Darwin
runners.spotlight: Made a winning handicap debut in 1m turf contest last August; far from solid on subsequent form, latterly over 1m2f, and doesn't appear to be crying out for this further step up in distance; blinkers replace the cheekpieces worn last time.
runners.comment: Far from solid on form since win last summer and has a stamina doubt

runners.name: Saxophonic
runners.spotlight: Good third over 1m2f at Newcastle (handicap debut; final start for Karl Burke) when last seen, shaping as if this longer trip will suit; still in same ownership; interesting, with another step forward possible.
runners.comment: Good third at Newcastle (handicap debut; final start for Karl Burke) when last seen

runners.name: Make It Up
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to showing some improvement with third-place finish at Wolverhampton (9.5f) on handicap/sesaonal debut, shaping as if this longer trip will suit; receives plenty of weight all round and could go well.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to improved effort at Wolverhampton on handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Rajbello
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden; only a modest fifth with blinkers fitted at Wolverhampton (9.5f) on handicap/seasonal debut but shaped as if this additional distance may prompt improvement; headgear retained.
runners.comment: This additional distance may prompt improvement in second handicap attempt

runners.name: Goldfinder
runners.spotlight: Has proved consistent in 1m AW maidens, finishing fourth each time; may take a step forward now handicapping and upped to a trip more in keeping with his pedigree.
runners.comment: May take a step forward now handicapping and upped in trip

runners.name: Tonyfromhr
runners.spotlight: Less solid than Goldfinder on 1m AW maiden form and, judged solely on breeding, less likely to be suited by this new trip to boot; gelded since last run and wears first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Holds weaker claims than fellow handicap debutant Goldfinder

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Fiftyshadesaresdev
runners.spotlight: Placed on numerous occasions, including at Southwell, but remains a longstanding maiden and he's been beaten around 12l in his two runs this year; blinkers return and cheekpieces are removed.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden and he's been beaten around 12l in his two runs this year

runners.name: Star Of Jupiter
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 17 starts but he reached the frame on numerous occasions over 2m/2m1f in July-October last year; ended 2025 on a low note but he's well handicapped on his best form and needs watching in market after five months off.
runners.comment: Plenty of good efforts over 2m/2m1f last year and he needs watching in market on return

runners.name: Parikarma
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner with three on the Flat and three over hurdles; returned after a lengthy absence in February but she's posted two respectable efforts over hurdles since; on dangerous mark back on the Flat but she's 0-21 on AW and others are preferred.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark back on the Flat but she's 0-21 on AW and others are preferred

runners.name: Hiccups
runners.spotlight: Beat a Catterick specialist by a neck (1m6f, good) in September before ending last season with solid efforts in his final two runs for John Berry; has claims if he can pick where he left off but he doesn't have a great record when fresh and still has something to prove on AW.
runners.comment: Doesn't have a great record when fresh and he has bit to prove back on AW for new yard

runners.name: Nymphaea
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent ten-race maiden but she was runner-up over C&D last month and wasn't beaten far back on turf at Beverley (1m4f) last time; tongue-tie is now added and she's in the mix back up in trip.
runners.comment: 0-10 but she was runner-up over C&D last month; in the mix with tongue-tie added

runners.name: Bronte Beach
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has not progressed and has form figures of 778 in three AW handicaps (1m/1m2f) this year; continues to drop down the weights but she needs a transformation at this new trip; hood added.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden and she needs a transformation at this new trip; hood added

runners.name: Angelardo
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt when forging clear at Newcastle (2m, Tapeta) last month but he's been well held on Polytrack and Tapeta since; has bit to prove again and he needs more progress in first-time blinkers; stablemate of Yakhabar.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle (2m) but well held in both runs since; needs more with blinkers added

runners.name: Cogital
runners.spotlight: Hard to predict and was out the back at Wolverhampton (1m4f) three weeks ago but he finished runner-up over hurdles at Chepstow (2m) last month and his last Flat win was off this mark (1m6f, good) last summer; comes with risks attached but he's not ruled out back up in trip; change of headgear.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but his last win was off this mark and he's not ruled out

runners.name: Lawmans Blis
runners.spotlight: Back to form with a second here (1m3f) in January but that hasn't been a springboard to better things; returns for new yard after wind surgery but his last win was 13 months ago and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Returns after wind surgery but he's hard to predict and others are more convincing

runners.name: Yakhabar
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 7yo who was well held here last time but that was after an emphatic win in an amateur jockeys' event at this track (1m4f) last month; has possibilities if he can recapture that form and he's effective at up to 2m; reapplied blinkers replace a visor.
runners.comment: Easy win here (1m4f) on his penultimate run and has claims if he can recapture that form

runners.name: Prince Abu
runners.spotlight: Triple course winner (1m6f/2m) but his last success was in December 2022 and he didn't pose a threat in just two runs last year; returns after 318 days off and he has some work to do back in trip; hood reapplied.
runners.comment: On long losing run and he has some work to do after 318 days off

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Recobella
runners.spotlight: Classified stakes win at Nottingham (1m2f, good; 10-1) last July, before going close in a Class 6 0-55 at Beverley (5-4 favourite) six days later; finished season more low-key, but her Doncaster comeback third (1m2f; 16-1) gives her a shout.
runners.comment: Classified stakes win at Nottingham (1m2f) last July; fair comeback third at Doncaster.

runners.name: Sisterandbrother
runners.spotlight: 6-53; with Shaun Harris when scoring in first-time cheekpieces at Kempton (1m4f; 5-1) in January, that his first win for a year; met traffic problems, seventh of 12, on recent stable debut at Beverley (1m4f; 3-1 favourite); fair mark, with headgear return the relevant angle.
runners.comment: Won in first-time cheekpieces at Kempton (1m4f) in January; excuse on recent stable debut.

runners.name: Le Pelerin
runners.spotlight: Maiden (0-11); twice placed on AW, including when third to Serious Look at Wolverhampton (9.4f; 10-1) last October; less effective since - eased 5lb in the weights - but could draw benefit from his Yarmouth comeback run (first outing since wind surgery).
runners.comment: Maiden (0-11); twice placed on AW, including over 9.4f at Wolverhampton; easing mark.

runners.name: Cloudside Rock
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough in a 0-55 at Ffos Las (1m4f, soft; 6-4 favourite) last August; failed to back that up in two subsequent starts, the latest when fifth of seven at Redcar (1m6f, good) in November; 2lb lower returned to AW (0-7) for seasonal reappearance.
runners.comment: Ffos Las winner (1m4f, soft) last August; failed to back up since; seasonal reappearance.

runners.name: The Bay Warrior
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner; has slipped to 6lb below his last winning mark (1m4f, Lingfield) and wasn't beaten far when having the run of the race under Rose Dawes at Wolverhampton (1m4f; third of seven, 7-2) last week; foolish to discount.
runners.comment: Not beaten far under Rose Dawes at Wolverhampton (1m4f; third) last week; isn't discounted

runners.name: Roman Secret
runners.spotlight: Overcame a wide draw to resume winning ways at Wolverhampton (9.4f; 22-1) a fortnight ago; that was her first win for 29 months and, while it's possible she'll strip fitter again, you couldn't be totally confident of a repeat from this 3lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Wolverhampton winner (9.4f; 22-1) two weeks ago; 3lb higher with that comeback run banked.
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Arranmore
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to score over C&D (16-1; from 3lb lower) five weeks ago; that came as a bit of a surprise, only his second win since August 2021, but the form was given a boost with the third (Moon Over The Sea) winning at Bath on Monday; needs considering.
runners.comment: C&D winner (16-1) five weeks ago; form franked since, so this 9yo does need considering.

runners.name: It's A Love Thing
runners.spotlight: 7-47 on AW but has struggled for form since finishing third at Newcastle (1m, Class 5; from 12lb higher) last November; failed to beat a rival in each of his last two starts (9.4f/1m4f) so it's difficult to have confidence.
runners.comment: Failed to beat rival in each of his last two starts (9.4f/1m4f); tough to have confidence

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pure Moon
runners.spotlight: Eye-catching second on his Nottingham debut (8.3f, heavy) in October and he built on that with a near-miss on his reappearance here (1m4f) 25 days ago; had some steering issues in that race but he finished well for a clear second and sets the standard on that form; key player.
runners.comment: Had near-miss on reappearance here this month, despite some steering issues; key player
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Lvii & Mrs G Jarvis

runners.name: Heroics
runners.spotlight: Showed promise with his late headway in two runs at Bath (1m, good to soft/soft) in October; should have more to offer over middle distances as a 3yo and he needs a close look on his return; gelded since his last run.
runners.comment: Two promising efforts at Bath in October and he needs a close look on return; been gelded

runners.name: Too Farhh Gone
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to some useful winners and she's out of a Listed-placed 1m2f winner (RPR 99); runner-up on Southwell debut (1m) in September and was a fair third at Wolverhampton (8.6f) two months later; needs to find improvement after five months off but it's still early days and this step up in trip could help; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Placed in her two runs in the autumn and she's in the mix upped in trip after a break

runners.name: Al Za'ama
runners.spotlight: Finished last on Bath debut (1m, good to soft; 4-1) in October, but after wind surgery he showed some ability when fourth at Kempton (1m, AW) in December; returns after another break but he has plenty of stamina in his pedigree and is open to more progress on this step up in trip; in the mix with tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Showed ability at Kempton and he's open to more progress upped to 1m3f on his return

runners.name: Picaro
runners.spotlight: Ran green on his Ripon debut (1m3f, good to soft) two weeks ago and he finished tailed off in fourth of seven; should be more streetwise this time but he needs a transformation.
runners.comment: Weak 14-1 shot and he ran green when tailed off on recent Ripon debut

runners.name: Alkaios
runners.spotlight: 7,000gns foal, 10,000gns yearling; third foal; gelded half-brother to 1m-1m3f winner Sea Spray (including Scandinavia; RPR 80); dam Listed-placed 1m2f/1m4f winner (103), out of 1m3f winning sister to German 1m6f Listed winner Poseidon Adventure; yard 0-15 in novice events last year and he's probably one for later on.
runners.comment: Yard 0-15 in novice events last year and he's probably one for later on

runners.name: Chilli King
runners.spotlight: Attracted support into 17-2 on recent Bath debut (1m2f, good) but he was always in rear and trailed home in a remote seventh of eight.
runners.comment: Attracted support into 17-2 on recent Bath debut but he trailed home in a remote seventh

runners.name: Moonrunner
runners.spotlight: Sixties Icon half-brother to three winners including Frenzified (9.5f-1m4f including AW/US; RPR 103); beaten 8l when a never-dangerous fourth at Wolverhampton (9.4f, Tapeta) in December but that was an encouraging start behind some useful rivals; should know more this time and he's a likely improver upped in trip after a break.
runners.comment: Showed promise at Wolverhampton and he's a likely improver upped in trip on his return

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap

runners.name: Jimmy Speaking
runners.spotlight: Well backed at Doncaster (7f Class 4, good to soft) just over a month ago and that support was justified, as he drew clear of his 15 rivals; 4lb higher but no issues with AW and this long home straight should play to his strengths; the one to beat and he has Silvestre De Sousa in the saddle.
runners.comment: Drew clear of 15 rivals at Doncaster (7f) one month ago; 4lb higher; the one to beat.

runners.name: Packetofbiscuits
runners.spotlight: C&D winner twice in February, before only just failing to catch the winner back here in hat-trick bid; work to do with Jimmy Speaking from Doncaster (fifth), with recent Thirsk effort - last of 12 - hardly a ringing endorsement.
runners.comment: C&D winner (Class 5) in February; work to do to with Jimmy Speaking from Doncaster (fifth)
runners.owner: A Pot To Pete In Syndicate

runners.name: King Of Ithaca
runners.spotlight: 6-31 on AW, four of those wins gained over C&D and the most recent a ready success from a 1lb lower mark last December; failed to settle on outer when only eighth of 12 (Jersey Maverick sixth, State of Madness ninth) at Kempton three weeks ago; others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Ready C&D winner (1lb lower) last December; poor at Kempton latest; others in better form.

runners.name: State Of Madness
runners.spotlight: Three wins (form figures 1121432) for Charlie Clover, ending his time with that trainer with a narrow defeat here (6f) in February; high in the weights for this trainer (0-2) as a result, only ninth of 12 after an early stumble at Kempton (7f) latest.
runners.comment: Won three times (form 1121432) for Charlie Clover; higher in weights for new trainer (0-2)
runners.owner: The Likely Lads & S & A Mares

runners.name: Commander Of Life
runners.spotlight: Last win came on Chelmsford Polytrack (7f; from 4lb lower) last October; kept in decent form (4224243) this year, a sticking-on third at Nottingham (8.5f, good) 12 days ago to Saturday's Ripon scorer Highland Olly; Paul Mulrennan is a noted booking.
runners.comment: Decent form in 2026; third at Nottingham latest to Saturday's Ripon scorer Highland Olly.

runners.name: Jersey Maverick
runners.spotlight: Sole win at Epsom (7f, good to firm; from 8lb lower) last July; fine on AW, as shown with near miss at Kempton (7f; beaten a neck) last October; sixth - faring best of the three involved in this race - in recent 7f Class 5 at Kempton; may strip fitter, with Mikkel Mortensen's 5lb claim handy.
runners.comment: Sixth - faring best of three in this race - in recent 7f Class 5 at Kempton; fitter today?
runners.owner: Tim Cottier, Clark, Spence And Fellowes

runners.name: Sunriseontheboyne
runners.spotlight: Won three on AW at Dundalk (7f-1m) for Mick Mulvany and ended his time for that County Meath handler with a heavy-ground win at Listowel (1m; 13-2) last September; low-key in three starts for Ben Haslam, last of 12 over 1m2f at Doncaster on latest start; mark is beginning to ease.
runners.comment: Won three at Dundalk (7f-1m, AW) for Mick Mulvany; low-key in three starts for Ben Haslam.
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Alondra
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been on Tapeta including two in a row at Wolverhampton (5f) in February; no impact in last two starts but she had some traffic issues at Wolverhampton last time and this step back up in trip is no problem; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Two wins at Wolverhampton (5f) in February and she could be dangerous back up in trip

runners.name: Station X
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been on turf but he's unexposed on AW and has finished close up over C&D and at Newcastle (5f) in his two runs this year; on same mark as last time and he should make another bold bid back up in trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed on AW and he's finished close up on Tapeta in his two runs this year; key player

runners.name: Beaujolais Nouveau
runners.spotlight: Ran well in face of a stiff task in a Carlisle Listed event last summer; backed that up with a close third in a Thirsk handicap (7f, good) but she's lost her way in three subsequent runs; should be fine on this drop back in trip but she needs a major revival on this second run after a break.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but she's been out of sorts in last three runs and needs a major revival

runners.name: Peter The Wolf
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 11th attempt when landing a Chepstow maiden (6f, soft) in September but that was a golden opportunity and he struggled back in a handicap in his final run last season; drops back in grade on his return but he has a patchy profile and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Has a patchy profile and sole win was in a small-field maiden; opposable on his return

runners.name: Stanley Spencer
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been on AW, two over C&D; has had some ups and downs since his last success in September but went very close at Kempton last month and runner-up off this mark over C&D last time; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who was runner-up off this mark here last time; respected

runners.name: Alpha Magic
runners.spotlight: Struggled in two of four runs as 3yo but won a Hamilton handicap (6f, good to soft) on penultimate start; lightly raced 4yo who is a half-brother to an AW winner; creditable fourth of six at Pontefract (6f, good) on Wednesday.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; won at Hamilton last June; creditable fourth at Pontefract on Wednesday

runners.name: Roberto Caro
runners.spotlight: Won a Racing League handicap off this mark last summer; three solid efforts this year, including a third at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta) last month and a close fourth in big field at Ripon two weeks ago; remains lightly raced on AW and he's a key player back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Three creditable efforts this year and he remains feasibly treated; respected back on AW

runners.name: Tough Enough
runners.spotlight: Won at Sandown (7f, good to soft) in August 2023 but unplaced in 13 starts since and last of 12 here (7f) in January; mark continues to fall but he has a lot to prove back in trip after a break; regular hood removed.
runners.comment: Unplaced in 13 starts since his last success and has a lot to prove after his break

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.name: Style King
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who got off the mark in a 6f handicap at Lingfield (AW) last September; similar form when second to an improving rival at Wolverhampton 13 days ago; easy lead unlikely but he is still considered.
runners.comment: 6f AW win last September; second to an improver 13 days ago; should give his running

runners.name: Initial Blue
runners.spotlight: Got the better of a rival who has won twice since when scoring at Kempton three weeks ago (6f); not in the same form at Nottingham last time but it was 5f and he had an inexperienced apprentice on board; high on the list back at 6f and returned to AW.
runners.comment: Kempton win this month has been well advertised by the 2nd; less good on turf since (5f)

runners.name: What What What
runners.spotlight: Game effort to win over C&D in February off 1lb lower; only run since came at Thirsk 12 days ago when easy to back and dropping right away in the race won by Eldeyaar; racecourse evidence suggests he will be much happier now back on AW.
runners.comment: C&D win in February; struggled, not for the first time on turf, 12 days ago; can rebound

runners.name: Velvet Skies
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on last year's Irish best; has not made a big impact in three runs for Stephen Hanlon (single-figure price on each occasion) and cheekpieces didn't help over 7f three weeks ago; dropping down the weights and new headgear is tried but there are risks attached.
runners.comment: Should be well treated but he is yet to shine for new yard; change of headgear today

runners.name: Spirit Of Jenny
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but she has shown plenty of promise, including when second of nine in a Class 5 fillies' handicap here 23 days ago (7f); forced a strong pace on that occasion and ran a good race in the circumstances; drops into her first Class 6 handicap and although she is a prominent racer, her best efforts have come when taking a lead; not fully exposed.
runners.comment: Good second over 7f here on her reappearance this month; drops in class; not fully exposed

runners.name: Eldeyaar
runners.spotlight: Responded well to a change of headgear when making all in a 17-runner handicap at Thirsk 12 days ago (6f, good); 6lb higher back on AW and he's not the only pace influence in the field.
runners.comment: Beat 16 rivals at Thirsk 12 days ago when tried in visor; up 6lb; other pace to deal with

runners.name: Anificas Beauty
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in three classified events in January; has more ability than her rating suggests but she doesn't help herself by pulling and her optimum trip isn't easy to gauge.
runners.comment: Unplaced in three classified events in January; drops in trip with a point to prove

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap

runners.name: Marra Donna
runners.spotlight: Consistent; ran best race last time when only just failing to reel in the winner at Lingfield (5f; in this newly applied equipment); no issue with this extra furlong and the form of that race was given a boost when the fourth won at Bath; possible.
runners.comment: Consistent; ran best race last time when only just failing to reel in winner at Lingfield.

runners.name: The Tunguska Event
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW at Newcastle (6f), keeping on well from 7lb lower on penultimate start; went down fighting at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) three weeks ago; Warren Fentiman's 3lb claim is helpful; player.
runners.comment: Went down fighting at Pontefract (6f) latest; Warren Fentiman's 3lb claim is helpful.

runners.name: Carnation Queen
runners.spotlight: Extended her trainer's fine record in the Rous Selling Stakes at York (6f, good to firm) when winning last June; not at her best on her final two starts last year, but more like it when fifth at Newcastle (6f AW; with Taaklam two places ahead) 17 days ago; stablemate of Dabbling.
runners.comment: York winner; fair fifth at Newcastle (6f, AW; with Taaklam two places ahead) 17 days ago.

runners.name: Taaklam
runners.spotlight: Ex-Ed Walker; couldn't cling on when third on stable debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) 17 days ago, with Carnation Queen fifth; a hood is now applied, presumably to curb a free-going nature; Harriet Bethell has been among the winners in recent weeks.
runners.comment: Ex-Ed Walker; couldn't cling on when 3rd on stable debut at Newcastle (6f); hooded today.

runners.name: Belling The Cat
runners.spotlight: Raised her game marginally since sent handicapping, runner-up to Ben Haslam's Liquid Cooled (who has previous with The Tunguska Event) at Newcastle (6f) on penultimate start; latest Redcar fourth was fine; stablemate of Jack Rabbit Slims.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Newcastle (6f AW) on penultimate start; stablemate of Jack Rabbit Slims.

runners.name: Obsidian Verse
runners.spotlight: Favourite for AW/handicap debut at Lingfield (7f; first run as a gelding) five weeks ago but never figured, finishing eighth of ten; 2lb lower and back over 6f, seeking something more positive.
runners.comment: Never figured on AW/handicap debut at Lingfield (7f; favourite) five weeks ago; 2lb lower.

runners.name: Jane Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over 5f here (20-1) three starts back; less productive at Wolverhampton (6f) and Newcastle (5f) since; maiden (0-9) with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 5f here (20-1); less productive since at Wolverhampton and Newcastle.

runners.name: Dabbling
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-5); entitled to strip fitter after recent comeback run in sixth of ten at Catterick (5f, good; 12-1); had a valid excuse - bled from the nose - when 2-1 favourite on her only other attempt over 6f; stablemate of Carnation Queen.
runners.comment: 0-5; entitled to strip fitter after recent comeback run in sixth of ten at Catterick (5f).

runners.name: Jack Rabbit Slims
runners.spotlight: 0-7; runner-up in Carlisle maiden (6f, soft) last June but has failed to kick on; finished eighth (two places behind Dabbling) on comeback run at Catterick (5f, good; 10-1) three weeks ago; stablemate of Belling The Cat.
runners.comment: 0-7; eighth (two places behind Dabbling) on recent comeback run at Catterick (5f, good).

runners.name: Perfect Price
runners.spotlight: No immediate progress for switch to handicapping, making only limited late headway at Newcastle (5f, AW; seventh of 11, 22-1) ten days ago; her dam won over 7f-1m, so this step back up in trip should aid her cause.
runners.comment: No immediate progress for switch to handicapping; step back up in trip should aid cause.

runners.name: Barry The Worm
runners.spotlight: Built on his Chelmsford third to score in newly applied cheekpieces at Wolverhampton (6f, AW; 11-1 from 4lb lower) last November; lacks the benefit of a recent run but there may be further improvement to come.
runners.comment: Scored in newly applied cheekpieces at Wolverhampton (6f) last November; lacks recent run.

runners.name: Inclusive
runners.spotlight: 0-6; some positives to take from running-on third over C&D on New Year's Day, but fewer at Lingfield next time; left James Fanshawe for 9,000gns the following month; best watched on stable debut off 14-week break.
runners.comment: Left J Fanshawe for 9,000gns in February; best watched on stable debut off 14-week break.
